drill_examples.pinyin: Shāfā zài kètīng de zhōngjiān.
drill_examples.translation_en: The sofa is in the middle of the living room.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 소파가 거실 중간에 있습니다.

negative_examples.error: Wrong order: facility must come before 在.

incorrect_examples.error: Do not use 有 with 在 for location; just say 冷氣在客廳.

transformation_drills: Change '冷氣在客廳' to question: '冷氣在哪裡？'
Change '洗衣機在陽台' to negative: '洗衣機不在陽台'
production_drills: Describe where facilities are in your apartment: '冰箱在廚房。'
Ask a partner: '你的書桌在哪裡？'
common_error_patterns: Using 有 with 在
Putting location before facility

repair_feedback.word_order: Facility first, then 在, then location.
repair_feedback.politeness: Neutral structure; no special politeness needed.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use location words like 客廳, 廚房, 陽台.

explanation_en: Use 壞掉了 to indicate that a facility is broken or not working.
title: 壞掉了 for Broken Facility
meaning: [Facility] is broken.
when_to_use: Reporting a problem with a facility
Complaining to a landlord or repair person
when_not_to_use: When the facility is just old but working
When describing a temporary issue like power outage
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, when something breaks, you say '壞掉了' to the landlord. It's common to add '要修理' (needs repair).
word_order_notes: Facility comes first, then 壞掉了. No need for 是 or 了 after 壞掉.
english_contrast: English says 'The air conditioner is broken.' Chinese says '冷氣壞掉了.'
korean_contrast: Korean uses '고장났어요' after the facility. Chinese uses 壞掉了.

correct_examples.text: 公寓的客廳很大，可是冷氣壞掉了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Gōngyù de kètīng hěn dà, kěshì lěngqì huài diào le.
correct_examples.translation_en: The living room of the apartment is very big, but the air conditioner is broken.

correct_examples.text: 房間的窗戶壞掉了，所以很熱。
correct_examples.pinyin: Fángjiān de chuānghù huài diào le, suǒyǐ hěn rè.
correct_examples.translation_en: The window in the room is broken, so it's very hot.

correct_examples.text: 廚房的冰箱壞掉了，裡面的肉都不新鮮了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Chúfáng de bīngxiāng huài diào le, lǐmiàn de ròu dōu bù xīnxiān le.
correct_examples.translation_en: The refrigerator in the kitchen is broken, so the meat inside is no longer fresh.

correct_examples.text: 浴室的馬桶壞掉了，需要請人修理。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yùshì de mǎtǒng huài diào le, xūyào qǐng rén xiūlǐ.
correct_examples.translation_en: The toilet in the bathroom is broken, and we need to ask someone to fix it.

correct_examples.text: 陽台的門壞掉了，所以我們不能出去。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yángtái de mén huài diào le, suǒyǐ wǒmen bù néng chūqù.
correct_examples.translation_en: The door to the balcony is broken, so we can't go out.

correct_examples.text: 客廳的燈壞掉了，晚上很暗。
correct_examples.pinyin: Kètīng de dēng huài diào le, wǎnshàng hěn àn.
correct_examples.translation_en: The light in the living room is broken, so it's very dark at night.

correct_examples.text: 房間的冷氣壞掉了，房東說明天會來修理。
correct_examples.pinyin: Fángjiān de lěngqì huài diào le, fángdōng shuō míngtiān huì lái xiūlǐ.
correct_examples.translation_en: The air conditioner in the room is broken, and the landlord said he will come to fix it tomorrow.

correct_examples.text: 廚房的熱水器壞掉了，不能洗澡。
correct_examples.pinyin: Chúfáng de rèshuǐqì huài diào le, bù néng xǐzǎo.
correct_examples.translation_en: The water heater in the kitchen is broken, so we can't take a shower.

drill_examples.text: 公寓的客廳很大，可是冷氣壞掉了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Gōngyù de kètīng hěn dà, kěshì lěngqì huài diào le.
drill_examples.translation_en: The living room of the apartment is very big, but the air conditioner is broken.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 아파트 거실은 매우 넓지만 에어컨이 고장 났어요.

drill_examples.text: 房間的窗戶壞掉了，所以很熱。
drill_examples.pinyin: Fángjiān de chuānghù huài diào le, suǒyǐ hěn rè.
drill_examples.translation_en: The window in the room is broken, so it's very hot.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 방의 창문이 고장 나서 매우 더워요.

drill_examples.text: 廚房的冰箱壞掉了，裡面的肉都不新鮮了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Chúfáng de bīngxiāng huài diào le, lǐmiàn de ròu dōu bù xīnxiān le.
drill_examples.translation_en: The refrigerator in the kitchen is broken, so the meat inside is no longer fresh.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 부엌의 냉장고가 고장 나서 안에 있는 고기가 모두 신선하지 않아요.

drill_examples.text: 浴室的馬桶壞掉了，需要請人修理。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yùshì de mǎtǒng huài diào le, xūyào qǐng rén xiūlǐ.
drill_examples.translation_en: The toilet in the bathroom is broken, and we need to ask someone to fix it.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 욕실의 변기가 고장 나서 수리공을 불러야 해요.

drill_examples.text: 陽台的門壞掉了，所以我們不能出去。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yángtái de mén huài diào le, suǒyǐ wǒmen bù néng chūqù.
drill_examples.translation_en: The door to the balcony is broken, so we can't go out.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 발코니 문이 고장 나서 나갈 수 없어요.

drill_examples.text: 客廳的燈壞掉了，晚上很暗。
drill_examples.pinyin: Kètīng de dēng huài diào le, wǎnshàng hěn àn.
drill_examples.translation_en: The light in the living room is broken, so it's very dark at night.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 거실의 등이 고장 나서 밤에 매우 어두워요.

drill_examples.text: 房間的冷氣壞掉了，房東說明天會來修理。
drill_examples.pinyin: Fángjiān de lěngqì huài diào le, fángdōng shuō míngtiān huì lái xiūlǐ.
drill_examples.translation_en: The air conditioner in the room is broken, and the landlord said he will come to fix it tomorrow.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 방의 에어컨이 고장 났는데 집주인이 내일 와서 고치겠다고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 廚房的熱水器壞掉了，不能洗澡。
drill_examples.pinyin: Chúfáng de rèshuǐqì huài diào le, bù néng xǐzǎo.
drill_examples.translation_en: The water heater in the kitchen is broken, so we can't take a shower.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 부엌의 온수기가 고장 나서 샤워를 할 수 없어요.

negative_examples.error: Wrong order: facility must come before 壞掉了.

incorrect_examples.error: Do not use 是 with 壞掉了; just say 冷氣壞掉了.

transformation_drills: Change '冷氣壞掉了' to question: '冷氣壞掉了嗎？'
Change '冰箱壞掉了' to negative: '冰箱沒有壞掉'
production_drills: Report a problem: '熱水器壞掉了。'
Ask a partner: '你的房間有什麼壞掉了？'
common_error_patterns: Putting 壞掉了 before facility
Adding 是 unnecessarily

repair_feedback.politeness: Use 不好意思 before reporting to be polite: '不好意思，冷氣壞掉了。'
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use facility words like 冷氣, 熱水器, 馬桶.

explanation_en: Use this pattern to politely ask someone to do something for you. It is commonly used when requesting help from a landlord or repair person.
title: Polite Requests with 請幫我
meaning: Please help me do something
when_to_use: When asking a landlord to fix something
When requesting a repair person to check an issue
When needing assistance with a household problem
when_not_to_use: When giving a direct command without politeness
When the action is for yourself only
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, using 請幫我 is very polite and shows respect. It is common to add 麻煩你 for extra politeness in formal situations.
word_order_notes: The verb must come after 幫我, and the object after the verb. Do not place the object before 幫我.
english_contrast: In English, you might say 'Please help me fix the toilet.' In Chinese, the structure is fixed: 請幫我 + verb + object.
korean_contrast: In Korean, you might say '도와주세요' (please help me). In Chinese, 請幫我 is similar but requires a specific verb after 幫我.

correct_examples.text: 請幫我修理水龍頭。
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐng bāng wǒ xiūlǐ shuǐlóngtóu.
correct_examples.translation_en: Please help me fix the faucet.

correct_examples.text: 請幫我檢查馬桶。
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐng bāng wǒ jiǎnchá mǎtǒng.
correct_examples.translation_en: Please help me check the toilet.

correct_examples.text: 請幫我換燈泡。
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐng bāng wǒ huàn dēngpào.
correct_examples.translation_en: Please help me change the light bulb.

correct_examples.text: 請幫我看看冷氣的問題。
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐng bāng wǒ kàn kàn lěngqì de wèntí.

repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use specific verbs like 修理, 檢查, 換 for repair situations.

pattern: 什麼時候 + 可以 + verb
explanation_en: Use this pattern to ask when something can be done. It is useful for scheduling repairs or visits with a landlord or repair person.
title: Asking About Time with 什麼時候可以
meaning: When can (someone) do something?
when_to_use: When asking when a repair person can come
When asking when the landlord can check something
When scheduling a visit
when_not_to_use: When asking about past events
When the subject is not clear from context
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, this is a polite way to ask about availability. You can add 你 or 您 for clarity, but it is often omitted if the context is clear.
word_order_notes: 什麼時候 comes first, followed by 可以 and then the verb. Do not place the verb before 可以.
english_contrast: In English, you might say 'When can you come?' In Chinese, the structure is '什麼時候可以 + verb'.
korean_contrast: In Korean, you might say '언제 올 수 있어요?' (When can you come?). In Chinese, the structure is similar but uses 可以 instead of a separate verb for ability.

correct_examples.text: 師傅，請問你什麼時候可以過來看看？
correct_examples.pinyin: Shīfu, qǐngwèn nǐ shénme shíhou kěyǐ guòlái kànkan?
correct_examples.translation_en: Master, may I ask when you can come over to take a look?

correct_examples.text: 我什麼時候可以請你來修理馬桶？

correct_examples.text: 水龍頭壞掉了，你什麼時候可以來換？

correct_examples.translation_en: The water heater is making a strange noise; when can you come over to take a look?

correct_examples.text: 冰箱不冷了，你什麼時候可以來修理？

correct_examples.translation_en: The refrigerator is not cold anymore; when can you come to repair it?

pattern: 可能 + 是 + problem + 的 + 問題
explanation_en: Use this pattern to suggest a possible cause of a problem. It is useful when describing issues to a landlord or repair person.
title: Expressing Possible Causes with 可能是...的問題
meaning: It might be a problem with...
when_to_use: When guessing the cause of a leak
When suggesting what might be broken
When describing a strange sound or smell
when_not_to_use: When you are certain about the cause
When the problem is not related to a specific part
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, this pattern is used to politely suggest a cause without being too assertive. It shows you are open to the repair person's opinion.
word_order_notes: 可能 is followed by 是, then the specific problem, then 的, and finally 問題. Do not omit 的.
english_contrast: In English, you might say 'It might be a problem with the toilet.' In Chinese, the structure is '可能是 + problem + 的 + 問題'.
korean_contrast: In Korean, you might say '변기 문제일 수도 있어요' (It might be a toilet problem). In Chinese, the structure uses 可能 and 的 to connect the problem.

correct_examples.translation_en: It might be a problem with the water heater, so there's no hot water.

correct_examples.text: 可能是冰箱的問題，所以不冷。

explanation_en: Used to indicate that someone has experienced or done something before.
title: Experience with 過
meaning: Have done something before
when_to_use: When talking about past experiences
When asking if someone has ever done something
when_not_to_use: For actions that happened at a specific time (use 了 instead)
For ongoing or habitual actions
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 過 is commonly used in casual conversation to share experiences, e.g., '你去過台北嗎？'
word_order_notes: Place 過 immediately after the verb and before the object.
english_contrast: English uses 'have/has + past participle' (e.g., 'I have eaten'), while Chinese uses V + 過.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '-아/어 본 적이 있다' (e.g., '먹어 본 적이 있어요'), similar to V + 過 + 的 + 經驗.

drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ chī guò niúròumiàn, hěn hǎochī.
drill_examples.translation_en: I've had beef noodles; they're delicious.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 소고기 국수를 먹어 봤는데 아주 맛있어요.

drill_examples.text: 他看過那部電影嗎？
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā kàn guò nà bù diànyǐng ma?
drill_examples.translation_en: Has he seen that movie?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그 사람 그 영화 본 적 있나요?

drill_examples.text: 我們學過中文，可是不太會說。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xué guò Zhōngwén, kěshì bú tài huì shuō.
drill_examples.translation_en: We've studied Chinese, but we can't speak it very well.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 우리는 중국어를 배운 적이 있지만, 말을 잘 못해요.

drill_examples.text: 你喝過珍珠奶茶嗎？
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ hē guò zhēnzhū nǎichá ma?
drill_examples.translation_en: Have you ever had bubble tea?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 버블티 마셔 본 적 있나요?

drill_examples.text: 我從來沒有去過花蓮。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ cónglái méiyǒu qù guò Huālián.
drill_examples.translation_en: I've never been to Hualien.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 화롄에 가 본 적이 한 번도 없어요.

drill_examples.text: 她聽過這首歌，很喜歡。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā tīng guò zhè shǒu gē, hěn xǐhuān.
drill_examples.translation_en: She's heard this song and really likes it.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그녀는 이 노래를 들어 본 적이 있고 아주 좋아해요.

drill_examples.text: 你做過台灣小吃嗎？
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ zuò guò Táiwān xiǎochī ma?
drill_examples.translation_en: Have you ever made Taiwanese snacks?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 대만 간식을 만들어 본 적 있나요?

negative_examples.error: This implies experience, but '昨天' specifies a time; use 了 instead.

incorrect_examples.error: 過 must come after the verb, not before.

transformation_drills: Change '我吃牛肉麵' to express experience: '我吃過牛肉麵。'
production_drills: Ask a partner: '你吃過滷肉飯嗎？' and answer based on your experience.
common_error_patterns: Placing 過 before the verb
Using 過 with specific time expressions

repair_feedback.word_order: Remember: 過 goes right after the verb, like '吃過'.
repair_feedback.politeness: This structure is neutral; add '請問' for polite questions.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 過 with action verbs like 吃, 喝, 去, 看.

explanation_en: Used to describe an action that is currently in progress.
title: Ongoing Action with 正在
meaning: Is doing something right now
when_to_use: When describing what someone is doing at this moment
When answering '你在做什麼？'
when_not_to_use: For habitual actions (use 每天 or 常常)
For completed actions
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 正在 is common in both formal and casual speech; sometimes shortened to 在 in quick conversation.
word_order_notes: Place 正在 before the verb; the object comes after the verb.
english_contrast: English uses 'am/is/are + verb-ing' (e.g., 'I am eating'), while Chinese uses 正在 + V.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '-고 있다' (e.g., '먹고 있어요'), similar to 正在 + V.

correct_examples.text: 我正在看書，等一下再跟你聊天。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ zhèngzài kàn shū, děng yīxià zài gēn nǐ liáotiān.
correct_examples.translation_en: I am reading a book; I'll chat with you later.

correct_examples.text: 他正在吃早餐，所以現在不能接電話。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài chī zǎocān, suǒyǐ xiànzài bù néng jiē diànhuà.
correct_examples.translation_en: He is eating breakfast, so he can't answer the phone right now.

correct_examples.text: 媽媽正在廚房煮飯，我們先等一下。
correct_examples.pinyin: Māma zhèngzài chúfáng zhǔ fàn, wǒmen xiān děng yīxià.
correct_examples.translation_en: Mom is cooking in the kitchen; let's wait a moment first.

correct_examples.text: 妹妹正在房間裡做功課，不要吵她。
correct_examples.pinyin: Mèimei zhèngzài fángjiān lǐ zuò gōngkè, bùyào chǎo tā.
correct_examples.translation_en: My younger sister is doing homework in her room; don't disturb her.

correct_examples.text: 我們正在等公車，今天好像比較慢。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhèngzài děng gōngchē, jīntiān hǎoxiàng bǐjiào màn.
correct_examples.translation_en: We are waiting for the bus; it seems slower today.

correct_examples.text: 哥哥正在看電影，我們不要打擾他。
correct_examples.pinyin: Gēge zhèngzài kàn diànyǐng, wǒmen bùyào dǎrǎo tā.
correct_examples.translation_en: My older brother is watching a movie; let's not bother him.

correct_examples.text: 她正在聽音樂，所以沒聽到你叫她。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài tīng yīnyuè, suǒyǐ méi tīng dào nǐ jiào tā.
correct_examples.translation_en: She is listening to music, so she didn't hear you calling her.

correct_examples.text: 爸爸正在公司開會，我們晚一點再打電話。
correct_examples.pinyin: Bàba zhèngzài gōngsī kāihuì, wǒmen wǎn yīdiǎn zài dǎ diànhuà.
correct_examples.translation_en: Dad is in a meeting at the company; let's call him a bit later.

drill_examples.text: 我正在看書，等一下再跟你聊天。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ zhèngzài kàn shū, děng yīxià zài gēn nǐ liáotiān.
drill_examples.translation_en: I am reading a book; I'll chat with you later.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 지금 책을 보고 있어요. 잠시 후에 당신과 이야기할게요.

drill_examples.text: 他正在吃早餐，所以現在不能接電話。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài chī zǎocān, suǒyǐ xiànzài bù néng jiē diànhuà.
drill_examples.translation_en: He is eating breakfast, so he can't answer the phone right now.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 지금 아침을 먹고 있어서, 지금 전화를 받을 수 없어요.

drill_examples.text: 媽媽正在廚房煮飯，我們先等一下。
drill_examples.pinyin: Māma zhèngzài chúfáng zhǔ fàn, wǒmen xiān děng yīxià.
drill_examples.translation_en: Mom is cooking in the kitchen; let's wait a moment first.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 엄마가 지금 부엌에서 밥을 짓고 계세요. 우리 잠시 기다려요.

drill_examples.text: 妹妹正在房間裡做功課，不要吵她。
drill_examples.pinyin: Mèimei zhèngzài fángjiān lǐ zuò gōngkè, bùyào chǎo tā.
drill_examples.translation_en: My younger sister is doing homework in her room; don't disturb her.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 여동생이 방에서 숙제를 하고 있어요. 방해하지 마세요.

drill_examples.text: 我們正在等公車，今天好像比較慢。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhèngzài děng gōngchē, jīntiān hǎoxiàng bǐjiào màn.
drill_examples.translation_en: We are waiting for the bus; it seems slower today.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 우리는 지금 버스를 기다리고 있어요. 오늘은 좀 느린 것 같아요.

drill_examples.text: 哥哥正在看電影，我們不要打擾他。
drill_examples.pinyin: Gēge zhèngzài kàn diànyǐng, wǒmen bùyào dǎrǎo tā.
drill_examples.translation_en: My older brother is watching a movie; let's not bother him.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 오빠가 지금 영화를 보고 있어요. 우리 방해하지 말아요.

drill_examples.text: 她正在聽音樂，所以沒聽到你叫她。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài tīng yīnyuè, suǒyǐ méi tīng dào nǐ jiào tā.
drill_examples.translation_en: She is listening to music, so she didn't hear you calling her.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그녀는 지금 음악을 듣고 있어서, 당신이 부르는 소리를 듣지 못했어요.

drill_examples.text: 爸爸正在公司開會，我們晚一點再打電話。
drill_examples.pinyin: Bàba zhèngzài gōngsī kāihuì, wǒmen wǎn yīdiǎn zài dǎ diànhuà.
drill_examples.translation_en: Dad is in a meeting at the company; let's call him a bit later.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 아빠가 지금 회사에서 회의 중이세요. 우리 조금 이따 전화해요.

negative_examples.error: 正在 is for current actions, not habits; use '每天' with simple present.

incorrect_examples.error: Object should come after the verb, not before.

transformation_drills: Change '我吃早餐' to show ongoing action: '我正在吃早餐。'
production_drills: Describe what you are doing now: '我正在看書。'
common_error_patterns: Using 正在 with habitual actions
Placing object before verb

repair_feedback.word_order: Put 正在 before the verb, like '我正在吃'.
repair_feedback.politeness: This is neutral; add '不好意思' if interrupting.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use with action verbs like 吃, 喝, 看, 聽.

explanation_en: Used to indicate that an action has been completed.
title: Completed Action with 了
meaning: Has done something
when_to_use: When talking about actions that are finished
When answering '你吃了嗎？'
when_not_to_use: For ongoing actions (use 正在)
For future actions
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 了 is very common in daily speech; often used with time words like '昨天' or '剛才'.
word_order_notes: Place 了 after the verb and before the object.
english_contrast: English uses past tense (e.g., 'I ate'), while Chinese uses V + 了.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '-았/었다' (e.g., '먹었어요'), similar to V + 了.

negative_examples.error: 了 is for completed actions; '明天' is future, so use '會' instead.

incorrect_examples.error: 了 must come after the verb, not before.

transformation_drills: Change '我吃早餐' to show completion: '我吃了早餐。'
production_drills: Tell your partner what you ate today: '我吃了牛肉麵。'
common_error_patterns: Using 了 with future time
Placing 了 before the verb

repair_feedback.word_order: Put 了 after the verb, like '吃了'.
repair_feedback.politeness: This is neutral; use '請問' for polite questions.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use with action verbs like 吃, 喝, 去, 買.

explanation_en: Use 發生 to describe an event or incident that happened, often unexpected or problematic.
title: Using 發生 to Describe an Incident
meaning: Something happened (often unexpectedly)
when_to_use: When explaining an accident or problem
When describing an unexpected event
when_not_to_use: Do not use for planned events (use 有 or 舉行 instead)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 發生 is common in daily conversation to report issues like traffic accidents or misunderstandings.
word_order_notes: 發生 is a transitive verb; the event/incident is the object, placed after 發生.
english_contrast: English uses 'happen' or 'occur' with the event as subject (e.g., 'An accident happened'). Chinese places the event as object after 發生.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '일어나다' or '발생하다' with subject marker; Chinese uses 發生 as verb with object.

correct_examples.text: 昨天發生了一件糟糕的事情，我的手機壞掉了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Zuótiān fāshēng le yī jiàn zāogāo de shìqíng, wǒ de shǒujī huài diào le.
correct_examples.translation_en: A terrible thing happened yesterday—my phone broke.

correct_examples.text: 因為塞車，所以我今天上班遲到了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yīnwèi sāichē, suǒyǐ wǒ jīntiān shàngbān chídào le.
correct_examples.translation_en: Because of traffic, I was late for work today.

correct_examples.text: 他忘記帶鑰匙，結果進不了家門。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā wàngjì dài yàoshi, jiéguǒ jìn bù liǎo jiāmén.
correct_examples.translation_en: He forgot to bring his keys, so he couldn't get into the house.

correct_examples.text: 昨天突然下大雨，我沒有帶傘，所以全身都濕了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Zuótiān tūrán xià dàyǔ, wǒ méiyǒu dài sǎn, suǒyǐ quánshēn dōu shī le.
correct_examples.translation_en: It suddenly rained heavily yesterday, and I didn't bring an umbrella, so I got completely soaked.

correct_examples.text: 我的電腦壞掉了，結果今天的工作都做不完。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ de diànnǎo huài diào le, jiéguǒ jīntiān de gōngzuò dōu zuò bù wán.
correct_examples.translation_en: My computer broke, so I couldn't finish today's work.

correct_examples.text: 他因為感冒，所以今天沒有來上課。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā yīnwèi gǎnmào, suǒyǐ jīntiān méiyǒu lái shàngkè.
correct_examples.translation_en: He didn't come to class today because he has a cold.

correct_examples.text: 我弄丟了悠遊卡，結果要重新買一張。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ nòngdiū le yōuyóukǎ, jiéguǒ yào chóngxīn mǎi yī zhāng.
correct_examples.translation_en: I lost my EasyCard, so I had to buy a new one.

correct_examples.text: 昨天颱風來了，所以很多商店都沒有開。
correct_examples.pinyin: Zuótiān táifēng lái le, suǒyǐ hěnduō shāngdiàn dōu méiyǒu kāi.
correct_examples.translation_en: A typhoon came yesterday, so many shops were closed.

drill_examples.text: 昨天發生了一件糟糕的事情，我的手機壞掉了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Zuótiān fāshēng le yī jiàn zāogāo de shìqíng, wǒ de shǒujī huài diào le.
drill_examples.translation_en: A terrible thing happened yesterday—my phone broke.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 어제 끔찍한 일이 생겼어요. 제 휴대폰이 고장 났어요.

drill_examples.text: 因為塞車，所以我今天上班遲到了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yīnwèi sāichē, suǒyǐ wǒ jīntiān shàngbān chídào le.
drill_examples.translation_en: Because of traffic, I was late for work today.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 교통 체증 때문에 오늘 출근에 지각했어요.

drill_examples.text: 他忘記帶鑰匙，結果進不了家門。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā wàngjì dài yàoshi, jiéguǒ jìn bù liǎo jiāmén.
drill_examples.translation_en: He forgot to bring his keys, so he couldn't get into the house.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그가 열쇠를 가져오는 걸 잊어버려서 집에 들어갈 수 없었어요.

drill_examples.text: 昨天突然下大雨，我沒有帶傘，所以全身都濕了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Zuótiān tūrán xià dàyǔ, wǒ méiyǒu dài sǎn, suǒyǐ quánshēn dōu shī le.
drill_examples.translation_en: It suddenly rained heavily yesterday, and I didn't bring an umbrella, so I got completely soaked.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 어제 갑자기 큰 비가 내렸는데 우산을 안 가져와서 온몸이 다 젖었어요.

drill_examples.text: 我的電腦壞掉了，結果今天的工作都做不完。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ de diànnǎo huài diào le, jiéguǒ jīntiān de gōngzuò dōu zuò bù wán.
drill_examples.translation_en: My computer broke, so I couldn't finish today's work.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 제 컴퓨터가 고장 나서 오늘 일을 다 끝낼 수 없었어요.

drill_examples.text: 他因為感冒，所以今天沒有來上課。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā yīnwèi gǎnmào, suǒyǐ jīntiān méiyǒu lái shàngkè.
drill_examples.translation_en: He didn't come to class today because he has a cold.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 감기에 걸려서 오늘 수업에 오지 않았어요.

drill_examples.text: 我弄丟了悠遊卡，結果要重新買一張。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ nòngdiū le yōuyóukǎ, jiéguǒ yào chóngxīn mǎi yī zhāng.
drill_examples.translation_en: I lost my EasyCard, so I had to buy a new one.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 유유카드를 잃어버려서 새로 한 장 사야 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 昨天颱風來了，所以很多商店都沒有開。
drill_examples.pinyin: Zuótiān táifēng lái le, suǒyǐ hěnduō shāngdiàn dōu méiyǒu kāi.
drill_examples.translation_en: A typhoon came yesterday, so many shops were closed.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 어제 태풍이 와서 많은 가게들이 문을 열지 않았어요.

negative_examples.error: This is actually correct; no negative example needed here.

incorrect_examples.error: The event must be the object after 發生, not the subject.

transformation_drills: Change '車禍' to '地震' in '昨天發生車禍'
production_drills: Describe a small accident that happened yesterday using 發生
common_error_patterns: Placing the event before 發生 as subject

repair_feedback.word_order: The event should come after 發生, not before.
repair_feedback.politeness: N/A
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use specific event nouns like 車禍, 地震, 問題.

explanation_en: Use 結果 to introduce the result or outcome of a situation, often unexpected or contrasting with expectations.
title: Using 結果 to Show Outcome
meaning: The result was... (often unexpected)
when_to_use: When explaining the outcome of an event
When the result is surprising or contrary to expectation
when_not_to_use: Do not use for simple cause-effect (use 所以 instead)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 結果 is very common in storytelling to highlight an unexpected twist.
word_order_notes: 結果 is placed after the subject and before the clause describing the outcome.
english_contrast: English uses 'as a result' or 'it turned out that' often at the beginning. Chinese uses 結果 after the subject.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '결과' as a noun with particle; Chinese uses 結果 as a conjunction-like word.

correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ wàngjì dài yàoshi, jiéguǒ jìn bù liǎo jiā mén.
correct_examples.translation_en: I forgot to bring my keys, and as a result, I couldn't get into the house.

correct_examples.text: 他生病了，結果沒去上班。

pattern: 因為 + Reason + 所以 + Result
explanation_en: Use 因為...所以... to clearly state cause and effect, especially when explaining problems and their reasons.
title: Cause and Effect with 因為...所以...
meaning: Because... therefore...
when_to_use: When explaining why something happened
When giving a reason for a problem
when_not_to_use: Do not use when the reason is obvious or already known
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, this structure is very common in both formal and informal contexts for clear explanation.
word_order_notes: 因為 introduces the reason clause, 所以 introduces the result clause. The order is fixed.
english_contrast: English can reverse order (result + because). Chinese must follow 因為...所以... order.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '~기 때문에' or '~아서/어서' with different particles; Chinese uses two separate clauses.

pattern: Subject + 可以幫我 + Verb + Object + 嗎？
explanation_en: Use this pattern to politely ask someone to do something for you. It's a common and polite way to request help in Taiwan.
title: Polite Request for Help
meaning: Can you help me...?
when_to_use: When you need someone to perform an action for you
In service situations like asking a store clerk or a friend
when_not_to_use: When the request is very urgent or formal
When speaking to a superior in a very formal context
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, adding 嗎 at the end makes the request softer and more polite. It's commonly used in daily interactions.
word_order_notes: The verb directly follows 幫我, and the object comes after the verb.
english_contrast: In English, we say 'Can you help me + verb?' but in Chinese, the structure is 'Can help me + verb + object?'
korean_contrast: In Korean, you might say '도와주세요' but in Chinese, you specify the action after 幫我.

drill_examples.translation_en: I have a stomachache, can you help me buy medicine?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 배가 아픈데, 약 좀 사주실 수 있나요?

negative_examples.error: Missing 可以 before 幫我 to make it a polite request.

incorrect_examples.error: Word order is wrong; 你 should come before 可以幫我.

transformation_drills: Change '你幫我找手機嗎？' to '你可以幫我找手機嗎？'
production_drills: Ask a friend to help you find your phone.
common_error_patterns: Omitting 可以
Placing 你 after 幫我

repair_feedback.word_order: Remember: Subject + 可以幫我 + Verb + Object + 嗎
repair_feedback.politeness: Adding 可以 makes the request more polite.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 幫我 for 'help me'.

values.meaning_en: to break down, to be broken

explanation_en: Use this pattern to express a need or necessity. It's straightforward and commonly used in Taiwan.
title: Expressing Need
meaning: I need to...
when_to_use: When stating a necessity
In both formal and informal contexts
when_not_to_use: When making a polite request (use 可以幫我 instead)
When expressing desire (use 想 instead)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 需要 is neutral and can be used in many situations, from daily needs to formal requirements.
word_order_notes: 需要 is followed directly by a verb or a noun phrase.
english_contrast: In English, 'need' can be followed by a noun or verb, same as Chinese.
korean_contrast: In Korean, '필요하다' is used similarly, but Chinese uses 需要 directly before the action.

correct_examples.text: 我的手機壞掉了，我需要找人修理。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ de shǒujī huài diào le, wǒ xūyào zhǎo rén xiūlǐ.
correct_examples.translation_en: My phone is broken, I need to find someone to fix it.

correct_examples.text: 廁所漏水了，我需要請師傅來檢查。
correct_examples.pinyin: Cèsuǒ lòu shuǐ le, wǒ xūyào qǐng shīfù lái jiǎnchá.
correct_examples.translation_en: The toilet is leaking, I need to ask a repairman to check it.

correct_examples.text: 家裡跳電了，我需要幫忙處理。
correct_examples.pinyin: Jiā lǐ tiào diàn le, wǒ xūyào bāngmáng chǔlǐ.
correct_examples.translation_en: There's a power outage at home, I need help dealing with it.

correct_examples.text: 我把鑰匙弄丟了，需要打電話給房東。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ bǎ yàoshi nòng diū le, xūyào dǎ diànhuà gěi fángdōng.
correct_examples.translation_en: I lost my keys, I need to call the landlord.

correct_examples.text: 水槽堵住了，我需要請師傅來修理。
correct_examples.pinyin: Shuǐcáo dǔ zhù le, wǒ xūyào qǐng shīfù lái xiūlǐ.
correct_examples.translation_en: The sink is clogged, I need to ask a repairman to fix it.

correct_examples.text: 我錯過了最後一班捷運，需要叫計程車回家。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ cuòguò le zuìhòu yī bān jiéyùn, xūyào jiào jìchéngchē huí jiā.
correct_examples.translation_en: I missed the last MRT, I need to call a taxi to go home.

correct_examples.text: 我的錢包弄丟了，需要去服務台問一下。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ de qiánbāo nòng diū le, xūyào qù fúwùtái wèn yīxià.
correct_examples.translation_en: I lost my wallet, I need to go to the service counter to ask.

correct_examples.pinyin: Tā shòu shāng le, xūyào mǎshàng sòng tā qù yīyuàn.
correct_examples.translation_en: He got injured, I need to take him to the hospital immediately.

values.meaning_en: to break down, to be broken

pattern: Subject + 可以 + Verb + Object + 嗎？ / Subject + 必須 + Verb + Object
explanation_en: Use 可以 to ask for permission or possibility, and 必須 to express obligation. In Taiwan, 可以 is softer and more polite for requests.
title: Permission vs. Obligation
meaning: Can I...? / I must...
when_to_use: Use 可以 to ask if something is allowed
Use 必須 to state a necessity or rule
when_not_to_use: Do not use 必須 for polite requests
Do not use 可以 for strong obligations
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 可以 is very common in service contexts, e.g., '我可以試穿嗎？' 必須 is more formal and direct.
word_order_notes: 可以 and 必須 come before the verb, and the object after the verb.
english_contrast: In English, 'can' and 'must' are similar, but Chinese uses 嗎 for questions with 可以.
korean_contrast: In Korean, '~해도 돼요?' is similar to 可以, and '~해야 해요' is similar to 必須.

correct_examples.text: 我的手機壞掉了，你可以幫我修理嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ de shǒujī huài diào le, nǐ kěyǐ bāng wǒ xiūlǐ ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: My phone is broken, can you help me fix it?

correct_examples.text: 你必須先檢查問題，然後再修理。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū xiān jiǎnchá wèntí, ránhòu zài xiūlǐ.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must first check the problem, then fix it.

correct_examples.text: 師傅，請問你可以幫我看一下漏水的地方嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Shīfù, qǐngwèn nǐ kěyǐ bāng wǒ kàn yīxià lòushuǐ de dìfang ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: Master, may I ask if you can take a look at the leaking place for me?

correct_examples.text: 我必須馬上處理這個問題，因為水一直流。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ bìxū mǎshàng chǔlǐ zhège wèntí, yīnwèi shuǐ yīzhí liú.
correct_examples.translation_en: I must deal with this problem immediately because the water keeps flowing.

correct_examples.text: 不好意思，我的馬桶堵住了，你可以幫我嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Bù hǎoyìsi, wǒ de mǎtǒng dǔ zhù le, nǐ kěyǐ bāng wǒ ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, my toilet is clogged, can you help me?

correct_examples.text: 你必須先打電話給師傅，然後等他來修理。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū xiān dǎ diànhuà gěi shīfù, ránhòu děng tā lái xiūlǐ.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must first call the repairman, then wait for him to come fix it.

correct_examples.text: 我的冰箱壞掉了，你可以幫我檢查一下嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ de bīngxiāng huài diào le, nǐ kěyǐ bāng wǒ jiǎnchá yīxià ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: My refrigerator is broken, can you help me check it?

correct_examples.text: 你必須先告訴我問題在哪裡，我才能幫你解決。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū xiān gàosu wǒ wèntí zài nǎlǐ, wǒ cáinéng bāng nǐ jiějué.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must first tell me where the problem is, then I can help you solve it.

drill_examples.text: 我的冰箱壞掉了，你可以幫我檢查一下嗎？
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ de bīngxiāng huài diào le, nǐ kěyǐ bāng wǒ jiǎnchá yīxià ma?
drill_examples.translation_en: My refrigerator is broken, can you help me check it?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 냉장고가 고장 났는데, 한번 확인해 주실 수 있나요?

negative_examples.error: This sounds like 'Must I help you find the phone?' which is too strong for a request.

incorrect_examples.error: Cannot use 可以 and 必須 together; choose one.

production_drills: Ask if you can use the phone.
Say you must find your phone.
common_error_patterns: Mixing 可以 and 必須
Using 必須 for polite requests

repair_feedback.politeness: Use 可以 for polite requests, 必須 for obligations.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Choose the correct modal verb based on context.

values.meaning_en: to break down, to be broken

pattern: 想 + 要 + 退貨 / 換貨
explanation_en: Use '想' to express a desire to return or exchange an item. This is a polite and direct way to state your intention in a store.
title: Expressing Desire to Return or Exchange
meaning: I want to return/exchange...
when_to_use: When you want to return a defective item
When you want to exchange for a different size or color
when_not_to_use: When you are just browsing
When you want to complain without taking action
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, it's common to start with '不好意思' or '請問' before this phrase to be polite. For example: '不好意思，我想要退貨。'
word_order_notes: The verb '想' comes before '要', and '退貨' or '換貨' is the object. No additional particles are needed.
english_contrast: In English, you might say 'I'd like to return this.' In Chinese, '想' softens the request, similar to 'would like'.
korean_contrast: In Korean, you might say '환불하고 싶습니다' (I want a refund). The structure '想 + 要' is similar to '~고 싶다'.

correct_examples.text: 我想要退貨，因為這個衣服尺寸不合。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng yào tuìhuò, yīnwèi zhège yīfú chǐcùn bù hé.

correct_examples.text: 這個手機壞掉了，我想要換貨。

correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, wǒ kěyǐ tuìhuò ma? Zhège bāobāo yǒu xiácī.
correct_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, can I return this? This bag has a defect.

correct_examples.text: 我想換貨，因為顏色不對，我要藍色的。

transformation_drills: Change '退貨' to '換貨' to express exchange.
Add '不好意思' at the beginning to make it more polite.
production_drills: You bought a shirt that is too small. What do you say to the clerk?
You received a broken cup. How do you tell the store you want to return it?
common_error_patterns: Placing '要' before '想'
Omitting '要' and just saying '我想退貨' (acceptable but less common)

repair_feedback.politeness: Add '不好意思' at the beginning for politeness.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use '退貨' for return and '換貨' for exchange.

explanation_en: Use '有' to indicate that an item has a problem or defect. This is a common way to explain why you want to return or exchange something.
title: Describing a Problem with an Item
meaning: There is a problem/defect...
when_to_use: When the item is damaged or defective
When the item doesn't work properly
when_not_to_use: When you simply changed your mind
When the item is fine but you don't want it
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, it's polite to explain the problem briefly. For example: '這個手機有問題，螢幕壞掉了。'
word_order_notes: The noun '問題' or '瑕疵' comes after '有'. You can add a specific description after it.
english_contrast: In English, you might say 'There's a problem with this.' In Chinese, '有' directly states existence.
korean_contrast: In Korean, you might say '문제가 있습니다' (There is a problem). The structure '有 + 問題' is similar.

correct_examples.text: 這個衣服有瑕疵，我想退貨。
correct_examples.pinyin: Zhège yīfu yǒu xiácī, wǒ xiǎng tuìhuò.
correct_examples.translation_en: This clothing has a defect; I want to return it.

correct_examples.text: 我的手機壞掉了，可以換貨嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ de shǒujī huài diào le, kěyǐ huànhuò ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: My phone is broken; can I exchange it?

correct_examples.text: 這個杯子破掉了，我要退錢。
correct_examples.pinyin: Zhège bēizi pò diào le, wǒ yào tuì qián.
correct_examples.translation_en: This cup is broken; I want a refund.

correct_examples.text: 這本書髒掉了，可以換一本新的嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū zāng diào le, kěyǐ huàn yī běn xīn de ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: This book is dirty; can I exchange it for a new one?

correct_examples.text: 我買的鞋子尺寸不合，想要退貨。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ mǎi de xiézi chǐcùn bù hé, xiǎng yào tuìhuò.
correct_examples.translation_en: The shoes I bought don't fit; I want to return them.

correct_examples.text: 這個顏色不對，我要換貨。
correct_examples.pinyin: Zhège yánsè bù duì, wǒ yào huànhuò.
correct_examples.translation_en: This color is wrong; I want to exchange it.

correct_examples.text: 你送錯東西了，我要退貨。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ sòng cuò dōngxi le, wǒ yào tuìhuò.
correct_examples.translation_en: You sent the wrong item; I want to return it.

correct_examples.translation_en: This bag has a problem; the zipper is broken.

negative_examples.error: The word order is incorrect. '有' should come before '問題'.

incorrect_examples.error: You should use either '問題' or '瑕疵', not both together.

transformation_drills: Replace '問題' with '瑕疵' to specify a defect.
Add a specific description like '螢幕壞掉了' after '問題'.
production_drills: Your new phone has a cracked screen. How do you describe the problem?
The shirt you bought has a stain. What do you say?
common_error_patterns: Using '是' instead of '有' (e.g., '是問題' is incorrect)
Forgetting to specify the problem after '有問題'

repair_feedback.politeness: It's polite to explain the problem clearly.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use '問題' for general problems and '瑕疵' for defects.

pattern: 可以 + 換貨 / 退貨 + 嗎
explanation_en: Use '可以...嗎' to politely ask if it's possible to exchange or return an item. This is a common question in stores.
title: Asking for Permission to Exchange or Return
meaning: Can I exchange/return...?
when_to_use: When you want to check the store's policy
When you are unsure if an exchange is allowed
when_not_to_use: When you are sure about the policy
When you are just stating your intention
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, it's common to add '請問' at the beginning. For example: '請問，我可以換貨嗎？'
word_order_notes: The verb '可以' comes before the action (換貨/退貨), and '嗎' is at the end to form a question.
english_contrast: In English, you might say 'Can I exchange this?' The structure is similar, but Chinese uses '嗎' for yes/no questions.
korean_contrast: In Korean, you might say '교환할 수 있나요?' (Can I exchange?). The structure '可以...嗎' is similar to '~할 수 있나요?'.

transformation_drills: Change '換貨' to '退貨' to ask about returns.

You want to return a defective phone. How do you ask if it's possible?

Placing '嗎' before the action

repair_feedback.politeness: Add '請問' at the beginning for politeness.

pattern: Subject + 要 + 預約 + Time + 的 + Noun
explanation_en: Use this pattern to express the intention to book an appointment for a specific time slot.
title: Booking an Appointment with 預約
meaning: To book an appointment for a specific time.
when_to_use: When you want to schedule a formal appointment
When specifying the time and purpose of the booking
when_not_to_use: For casual meetups with friends
When the time is not specified
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 預約 is commonly used for medical appointments, restaurant reservations, and service bookings. It's polite to specify the time clearly.
word_order_notes: Time phrase must come before the noun being booked.
english_contrast: English uses 'book an appointment for [time]', while Chinese places the time before the noun.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '약속을 예약하다' with time markers, similar to Chinese structure.

correct_examples.text: 我要預約明天下午三點的門診。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ yào yùyuē míngtiān xiàwǔ sān diǎn de ménzhěn.
correct_examples.translation_en: I want to book an appointment for tomorrow afternoon at 3 PM.

correct_examples.text: 請問，可以取消今天的預約嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, kěyǐ qǔxiāo jīntiān de yùyuē ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, can I cancel today's appointment?

correct_examples.text: 我想把預約時間改到下個星期二上午。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng bǎ yùyuē shíjiān gǎi dào xià ge xīngqí'èr shàngwǔ.
correct_examples.translation_en: I'd like to reschedule my appointment to next Tuesday morning.

correct_examples.text: 你已經約好了，要取消預約需要付取消費。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ yǐjīng yuē hǎo le, yào qǔxiāo yùyuē xūyào fù qǔxiāo fèi.
correct_examples.translation_en: You've already confirmed the appointment; canceling it requires a cancellation fee.

correct_examples.text: 他打電話來確認明天的預約時間。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā dǎ diànhuà lái quèrèn míngtiān de yùyuē shíjiān.
correct_examples.translation_en: He called to confirm tomorrow's appointment time.

correct_examples.text: 我們可以線上預約，也可以電話預約。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen kěyǐ xiànshàng yùyuē, yě kěyǐ diànhuà yùyuē.
correct_examples.translation_en: We can book online, or we can book by phone.

correct_examples.text: 因為臨時有事，所以我必須取消預約。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yīnwèi línshí yǒu shì, suǒyǐ wǒ bìxū qǔxiāo yùyuē.
correct_examples.translation_en: Because something came up, I have to cancel the appointment.

correct_examples.text: 請問，這個星期五下午還有空檔可以預約嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, zhège xīngqíwǔ xiàwǔ hái yǒu kòngdǎng kěyǐ yùyuē ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, are there any available slots for an appointment this Friday afternoon?

drill_examples.text: 我要預約明天下午三點的門診。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ yào yùyuē míngtiān xiàwǔ sān diǎn de ménzhěn.
drill_examples.translation_en: I want to book an appointment for tomorrow afternoon at 3 PM.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 내일 오후 3시 진료 예약을 하고 싶습니다.

drill_examples.text: 請問，可以取消今天的預約嗎？

drill_examples.translation_ko: 실례합니다, 오늘 예약을 취소할 수 있을까요?

drill_examples.text: 我想把預約時間改到下個星期二上午。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng bǎ yùyuē shíjiān gǎi dào xià ge xīngqí'èr shàngwǔ.
drill_examples.translation_en: I'd like to reschedule my appointment to next Tuesday morning.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 예약 시간을 다음 주 화요일 오전으로 변경하고 싶습니다.

drill_examples.text: 你已經約好了，要取消預約需要付取消費。
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ yǐjīng yuē hǎo le, yào qǔxiāo yùyuē xūyào fù qǔxiāo fèi.
drill_examples.translation_en: You've already confirmed the appointment; canceling it requires a cancellation fee.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 이미 예약이 확정되었으니, 취소하려면 취소 수수료를 내셔야 합니다.

drill_examples.text: 他打電話來確認明天的預約時間。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā dǎ diànhuà lái quèrèn míngtiān de yùyuē shíjiān.
drill_examples.translation_en: He called to confirm tomorrow's appointment time.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그가 내일 예약 시간을 확인하려고 전화를 했습니다.

drill_examples.text: 我們可以線上預約，也可以電話預約。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen kěyǐ xiànshàng yùyuē, yě kěyǐ diànhuà yùyuē.
drill_examples.translation_en: We can book online, or we can book by phone.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 온라인으로 예약할 수도 있고, 전화로 예약할 수도 있습니다.

drill_examples.text: 因為臨時有事，所以我必須取消預約。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yīnwèi línshí yǒu shì, suǒyǐ wǒ bìxū qǔxiāo yùyuē.
drill_examples.translation_en: Because something came up, I have to cancel the appointment.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 갑작스러운 일이 생겨서 예약을 취소해야 합니다.

drill_examples.text: 請問，這個星期五下午還有空檔可以預約嗎？
drill_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, zhège xīngqíwǔ xiàwǔ hái yǒu kòngdǎng kěyǐ yùyuē ma?
drill_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, are there any available slots for an appointment this Friday afternoon?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 실례합니다, 이번 주 금요일 오후에 예약 가능한 시간이 있나요?

negative_examples.error: Time phrase should come before the noun, not after the verb.

incorrect_examples.error: Time phrase must be placed before the noun '門診'.

transformation_drills: Change '明天' to '下個星期' and adjust the sentence.
Change '門診' to '牙醫' and rephrase.
production_drills: Book an appointment for next Monday morning.
Book a haircut for this Friday afternoon.
common_error_patterns: Placing time after the noun
Omitting 的 between time and noun

repair_feedback.word_order: Put the time before the noun, e.g., '明天' before '門診'.
repair_feedback.politeness: Use 請 or 麻煩 for polite requests.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 預約 for formal bookings, not 約.

values.meaning_en: to make an appointment; to reserve
to reschedule; to change the date
to register (at a hospital/clinic); to check in

pattern: Subject + 想 + 改期 + 到 + New Time
explanation_en: Use this pattern to express the desire to reschedule an appointment to a new time.
title: Rescheduling with 改期
meaning: To change the date of an appointment.
when_to_use: When you need to move an existing appointment to a different time
When informing the service provider of the change
when_not_to_use: For canceling an appointment entirely
For casual plans with friends
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 改期 is polite and direct. It's common to apologize briefly before stating the change, e.g., '不好意思，我想改期。'
word_order_notes: The new time follows 到, indicating the target time.
english_contrast: English uses 'reschedule to [time]', while Chinese uses '改期到[time]'.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '날짜를 변경하다' with '로' for the new time, similar to '到'.

correct_examples.text: 我想改期到明天上午。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng gǎiqī dào míngtiān shàngwǔ.
correct_examples.translation_en: I want to reschedule to tomorrow morning.

correct_examples.text: 你已經預約了嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ yǐjīng yùyuē le ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: Have you already made an appointment?

correct_examples.text: 我想取消今天的預約。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng qǔxiāo jīntiān de yùyuē.
correct_examples.translation_en: I want to cancel today's appointment.

correct_examples.text: 請幫我確認預約時間。
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐng bāng wǒ quèrèn yùyuē shíjiān.
correct_examples.translation_en: Please help me confirm the appointment time.

values.meaning_en: to make an appointment; to reserve
to reschedule; to change the date
to register (at a hospital/clinic); to check in

pattern: Subject + 想 + 確認 + 預約 + 的 + Details
explanation_en: Use this pattern to confirm the details of an appointment, such as time, date, or location.
title: Confirming an Appointment with 確認
meaning: To verify the details of a booking.
when_to_use: When you want to double-check appointment details
When ensuring there are no misunderstandings
when_not_to_use: For making a new appointment
For casual inquiries
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 確認 is often used in service contexts. It's polite to confirm before the appointment day, e.g., '我想確認明天的預約。'
word_order_notes: The details (e.g., time, date) come after 的, modifying 預約.
english_contrast: English uses 'confirm the appointment for [details]', while Chinese places details after 的.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '예약을 확인하다' with details following, similar structure.

correct_examples.text: 我想確認明天的預約時間。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng quèrèn míngtiān de yùyuē shíjiān.
correct_examples.translation_en: I want to confirm the appointment time for tomorrow.

correct_examples.text: 他打電話來取消預約。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā dǎ diànhuà lái qǔxiāo yùyuē.
correct_examples.translation_en: He called to cancel the appointment.

correct_examples.text: 我們可以改期到下午嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen kěyǐ gǎiqī dào xiàwǔ ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: Can we reschedule to the afternoon?

correct_examples.text: 她想要確認醫院的門診時間。

correct_examples.text: 你必須提早到，不要遲到。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū tízǎo dào, bùyào chídào.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must arrive early; don't be late.

correct_examples.translation_en: I already made an online appointment and want to confirm it.

pattern: 如果 + condition + 的話，就 + action
explanation_en: Use '如果...的話，就...' to express 'if condition, then action'. This is the most common conditional structure in Chinese, similar to 'if...then...' in English.
title: 如果...的話，就... (If...then...)
meaning: If condition happens, then do action.
when_to_use: When making plans with a clear condition
When giving advice or suggestions
When discussing possible future situations
when_not_to_use: Do not use for hypothetical or impossible situations (use 要是 instead)
Do not use for past unreal conditions
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, '如果...的話' is very common in daily conversation. The '的話' part can be omitted in casual speech, but including it sounds more natural and polite.
word_order_notes: The condition clause always comes first, followed by the result clause. The subject can appear before or after 如果.
english_contrast: English uses 'if' at the beginning or middle; Chinese always places the condition first.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '-(으)면' at the end of the condition clause; Chinese uses '如果...的話' around the condition.

correct_examples.text: 如果下雨的話，我們就不要去公園。

common_error_patterns: Forgetting to use 就 in the result clause
Placing the result clause before the condition

repair_feedback.word_order: Remember: condition first, then result with 就.
repair_feedback.politeness: Using 的話 makes the sentence softer and more polite.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 如果 for real conditions; use 要是 for hypothetical ones.

explanation_en: Use '不然' or '否則' to mean 'otherwise' or 'or else', indicating a negative consequence if a condition is not met.
title: 不然 / 否則 (Otherwise)
meaning: Otherwise, a negative result will happen.
when_to_use: When warning someone about a consequence
When suggesting an alternative to avoid a problem
When explaining why a certain action is necessary
when_not_to_use: Do not use for positive alternatives (use 要不然 instead)
Do not use in formal writing without context
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, '不然' is more common in spoken language, while '否則' is more formal. Both are used to express 'otherwise'.
word_order_notes: The condition or suggestion comes first, followed by '不然/否則' and the negative result.
english_contrast: English uses 'otherwise' at the beginning of the consequence clause; Chinese uses it similarly.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '그렇지 않으면' which is similar in structure to Chinese '不然'.

drill_examples.text: 要是颱風來了的話，我們就取消行程。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yàoshì táifēng lái le de huà, wǒmen jiù qǔxiāo xíngchéng.
drill_examples.translation_en: If a typhoon comes, we will cancel the trip.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 태풍이 오면, 우리는 일정을 취소할 거예요.

drill_examples.text: 如果沒有帶傘，萬一下雨的話就會淋濕。
drill_examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ méiyǒu dài sǎn, wànyī xià yǔ de huà jiù huì lín shī.
drill_examples.translation_en: If you don't bring an umbrella, you'll get wet in case it rains.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 우산을 가져가지 않으면, 만약 비가 올 경우 젖을 거예요.

drill_examples.text: 你最好早點出門，否則的話會塞車。
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ zuìhǎo zǎo diǎn chūmén, fǒuzé de huà huì sāichē.
drill_examples.translation_en: You'd better leave early, otherwise there will be traffic jams.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 일찍 출발하는 게 좋아요, 그렇지 않으면 교통 체증이 있을 거예요.

drill_examples.text: 假如你不想吃牛肉麵，那麼我們就改吃水餃。
drill_examples.pinyin: Jiǎrú nǐ bù xiǎng chī niúròumiàn, nàme wǒmen jiù gǎi chī shuǐjiǎo.
drill_examples.translation_en: If you don't want to eat beef noodles, then we'll switch to dumplings.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 네가 소고기 국수를 먹고 싶지 않다면, 우리는 만두로 바꿀 거예요.

negative_examples.error: This sentence is correct. No error.

incorrect_examples.error: This sentence is correct. No error.

transformation_drills: Change '快點出門，不然會遲到' to '快點出門，否則會遲到'
Change '帶傘，不然會淋濕' to '帶傘，否則會淋濕'
production_drills: Make a sentence: study hard, otherwise you will fail
Make a sentence: bring a map, otherwise you will get lost
common_error_patterns: Using 不然 with a positive result
Confusing 不然 with 要不然

repair_feedback.word_order: Place 不然/否則 after the condition, before the negative result.
repair_feedback.politeness: Using 不然 is neutral; 否則 is more formal.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 不然 for spoken, 否則 for written.

explanation_en: Use '可能' to express possibility or probability, similar to 'might' or 'maybe' in English.
title: 可能 (Might / Maybe)
meaning: Indicates that something is possible or likely.
when_to_use: When speculating about future events
When expressing uncertainty
When making polite suggestions or guesses
when_not_to_use: Do not use for certain facts
Do not use with 一定 (definitely) in the same clause
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, '可能' is very common in daily conversation. It can be used at the beginning of a sentence or after the subject.
word_order_notes: 可能 usually comes before the verb, but can also be placed at the beginning of the sentence for emphasis.
english_contrast: English uses 'might' or 'maybe' before the verb; Chinese uses 可能 similarly.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '아마' or '~을 수도 있다' which is similar to Chinese 可能.

correct_examples.text: 如果下雨的話，我們就不要去公園了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ xià yǔ de huà, wǒmen jiù bù yào qù gōngyuán le.
correct_examples.translation_en: If it rains, we won't go to the park.

correct_examples.text: 要是你沒空的話，我就自己去買東西。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yàoshi nǐ méi kòng de huà, wǒ jiù zìjǐ qù mǎi dōngxī.
correct_examples.translation_en: If you're not free, I'll go shopping by myself.

correct_examples.text: 假如找不到鑰匙，就用備用鑰匙吧。
correct_examples.pinyin: Jiǎrú zhǎo bù dào yàoshi, jiù yòng bèiyòng yàoshi ba.
correct_examples.translation_en: If you can't find the keys, use the spare key.

correct_examples.text: 萬一塞車的話，我們可能會遲到。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wànyī sāichē de huà, wǒmen kěnéng huì chídào.
correct_examples.translation_en: In case of a traffic jam, we might be late.

correct_examples.text: 如果颱風來了，我們就待在家裡。
correct_examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ táifēng lái le, wǒmen jiù dāi zài jiā lǐ.
correct_examples.translation_en: If a typhoon comes, we'll stay at home.

correct_examples.text: 要是你覺得太貴，我們就換一個便宜的。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yàoshi nǐ juéde tài guì, wǒmen jiù huàn yī ge piányí de.
correct_examples.translation_en: If you think it's too expensive, we'll switch to a cheaper one.

correct_examples.text: 假如你忘了帶傘，我這裡有一把。
correct_examples.pinyin: Jiǎrú nǐ wàng le dài sǎn, wǒ zhèlǐ yǒu yī bǎ.
correct_examples.translation_en: If you forgot to bring an umbrella, I have one here.

correct_examples.text: 萬一沒趕上火車，就搭下一班吧。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wànyī méi gǎn shàng huǒchē, jiù dā xià yī bān ba.
correct_examples.translation_en: In case you miss the train, just take the next one.

explanation_en: Used to express that someone has experienced something in the past. It emphasizes the experience itself, not the specific time.
title: Past Experience with 曾經...過
meaning: Have done something before
when_to_use: When asking or talking about past experiences
When emphasizing that something happened at least once before
when_not_to_use: Do not use for actions that happened at a specific time (use 了 instead)
Do not use for actions that are still ongoing
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 曾經 is more formal than 有...過. In casual conversation, people often drop 曾經 and just use Verb + 過.
word_order_notes: 曾經 comes before the verb, and 過 comes after the verb. The object can come after 過 or before the verb.
english_contrast: English uses 'have/has + past participle' (e.g., 'I have eaten that before'). Chinese uses 曾經...過.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '-(어)본 적이 있다' (e.g., '먹어본 적이 있어요'). Chinese 曾經...過 is similar.

correct_examples.text: 我曾經去過台北，那裡的小吃很好吃。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ céngjīng qù guò Táiběi, nàlǐ de xiǎochī hěn hǎochī.
correct_examples.translation_en: I have been to Taipei before; the snacks there are delicious.

correct_examples.text: 他正在看書，你不要打擾他。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài kàn shū, nǐ bùyào dǎrǎo tā.
correct_examples.translation_en: He is reading a book; don't disturb him.

correct_examples.text: 你能夠幫我拿一下那個袋子嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ nénggòu bāng wǒ ná yīxià nàge dàizi ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: Can you help me grab that bag for a moment?

correct_examples.text: 與其坐公車，不如搭捷運比較快。

correct_examples.translation_en: Rather than taking the bus, it's faster to take the MRT.

correct_examples.text: 請你敘述一下昨天發生的事情。
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐng nǐ xùshù yīxià zuótiān fāshēng de shìqíng.
correct_examples.translation_en: Please describe what happened yesterday.

correct_examples.text: 這家餐廳的服務態度比那家好很多。

correct_examples.translation_en: The service attitude at this restaurant is much better than that one.

correct_examples.text: 我曾經學過中文，可是現在不太會說了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ céngjīng xué guò zhōngwén, kěshì xiànzài bù tài huì shuō le.
correct_examples.translation_en: I have studied Chinese before, but now I can't speak it very well.

correct_examples.text: 他正在修理他的腳踏車，需要幫忙嗎？

Forgetting 過 after the verb

repair_feedback.word_order: Remember: 曾經 + Verb + 過. Do not put 過 before the verb.
repair_feedback.politeness: Using 曾經 is polite and formal. In casual speech, you can just use Verb + 過.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 曾經 for notable experiences, not for daily routines.

values.meaning_en: not as good as; would rather

explanation_en: Used to describe an action that is currently in progress at the moment of speaking.
title: Ongoing Action with 正在
meaning: Is doing something right now
when_to_use: When describing what someone is doing at this moment
When answering 'What are you doing?'
when_not_to_use: Do not use for habitual actions (use 每天 or 常常 instead)
Do not use for future plans (use 要 or 打算 instead)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 正在 is more formal. In casual conversation, people often just use 在 alone (e.g., 我在吃飯).
word_order_notes: 正在 comes before the verb. The object comes after the verb.
english_contrast: English uses 'am/is/are + verb-ing' (e.g., 'I am eating'). Chinese uses 正在 + Verb.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '~고 있다' (e.g., '먹고 있어요'). Chinese 正在 is similar.

correct_examples.text: 他正在修理他的腳踏車。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài xiūlǐ tā de jiǎotàchē.

correct_examples.text: 我曾經去過台灣，但是沒有去過花蓮。

correct_examples.translation_en: I have been to Taiwan before, but I have never been to Hualien.

drill_examples.text: Wǒ nénggòu yòng yōuyóukǎ zuò jiéyùn, kěshì bù huì yòng xìnyòngkǎ.
drill_examples.pinyin: wo3 neng2gou4 yong4 you1you2ka3 zuo4 jie2yun4, ke3shi4 bu4 hui4 yong4 xin4yong4ka3.
drill_examples.translation_en: 저는 유유카로 지하철을 탈 수 있지만, 신용카드는 사용할 줄 몰라요.

drill_examples.text: 昨天下午我曾經去過那家咖啡店，可是現在已經關門了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Zuótiān xiàwǔ wǒ céngjīng qù guò nà jiā kāfēidiàn, kěshì xiànzài yǐjīng guānmén le.
drill_examples.translation_en: I went to that coffee shop yesterday afternoon, but it's already closed now.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 어제 오후에 그 카페에 가 본 적이 있는데, 지금은 이미 문을 닫았어요.

drill_examples.text: 他正在看書，所以我們不要打擾他。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài kàn shū, suǒyǐ wǒmen bù yào dǎrǎo tā.
drill_examples.translation_en: He is reading a book, so we shouldn't disturb him.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 책을 읽고 있어서 우리가 방해하지 말아야 해요.

drill_examples.text: 我能夠自己煮飯，可是不如我媽媽煮得好吃。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ nénggòu zìjǐ zhǔ fàn, kěshì bùrú wǒ māmā zhǔ dé hǎochī.
drill_examples.translation_en: I can cook by myself, but not as well as my mom.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 혼자서 밥을 할 수 있지만, 엄마가 하는 것만큼 맛있지는 않아요.

negative_examples.error: Cannot use 正在 with habitual actions. Use 每天 instead.

incorrect_examples.error: Cannot use 正在 for future plans. Use 要 or 打算 instead.

transformation_drills: Change '我吃飯。' to show ongoing action.
Change '他正在看書。' to a negative sentence.
production_drills: Tell your friend what you are doing right now using 正在.
Ask your classmate what they are doing at this moment.
common_error_patterns: Using 正在 for habitual or future actions
Forgetting the verb after 正在

repair_feedback.word_order: Remember: 正在 + Verb. Do not put the object before the verb.
repair_feedback.politeness: Using 正在 is polite. In casual speech, 在 is more common.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 正在 for actions happening right now, not for routines or plans.

values.meaning_en: not as good as; would rather

explanation_en: Used to express ability or capability to do something, often implying that the ability is acquired or that conditions allow it.
title: Ability with 能夠
meaning: Can / be able to
when_to_use: When talking about ability that requires effort or conditions
When emphasizing that someone has the capability to do something
when_not_to_use: Do not use for simple physical ability (use 會 instead)
Do not use for permission (use 可以 instead)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 能夠 is more formal than 會 or 可以. It is often used in written or formal speech.
word_order_notes: 能夠 comes before the verb. The object comes after the verb.
english_contrast: English uses 'can' or 'be able to' (e.g., 'I can speak Chinese'). Chinese uses 能夠 for ability with conditions.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '~ㄹ 수 있다' (e.g., '할 수 있어요'). Chinese 能夠 is similar.

correct_examples.text: 他正在修理他的腳踏車。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài xiūlǐ tā de jiǎotàchē.
correct_examples.translation_en: He is repairing his bicycle.

correct_examples.text: 我曾經去過台灣，但是沒有去過花蓮。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ céngjīng qù guò Táiwān, dànshì méiyǒu qù guò Huālián.
correct_examples.translation_en: I have been to Taiwan before, but I have never been to Hualien.

correct_examples.text: 我曾經能夠說流利的中文，可是現在不太會了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ céngjīng nénggòu shuō liúlì de zhōngwén, kěshì xiànzài bù tài huì le.
correct_examples.translation_en: I used to be able to speak fluent Chinese, but now I can't really.

correct_examples.text: 他正在修理他的腳踏車，所以不能跟我們一起去公園。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài xiūlǐ tā de jiǎotàchē, suǒyǐ bù néng gēn wǒmen yīqǐ qù gōngyuán.
correct_examples.translation_en: He is repairing his bicycle, so he can't go to the park with us.

correct_examples.translation_en: I went to that coffee shop yesterday afternoon, but it's already closed now.

correct_examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài kàn shū, suǒyǐ wǒmen bù yào dǎrǎo tā.
correct_examples.translation_en: He is reading a book, so we shouldn't disturb him.

drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 예전에 중국어를 유창하게 할 수 있었는데, 지금은 잘 못해요.

drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 자전거를 수리하고 있어서 우리랑 같이 공원에 갈 수 없어요.

drill_examples.translation_en: I went to that coffee shop yesterday afternoon, but it's already closed now.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 어제 오후에 그 카페에 가 본 적이 있는데, 지금은 이미 문을 닫았어요.

drill_examples.pinyin: Tā zhèngzài kàn shū, suǒyǐ wǒmen bù yào dǎrǎo tā.
drill_examples.translation_en: He is reading a book, so we shouldn't disturb him.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 책을 읽고 있어서 우리가 방해하지 말아야 해요.

drill_examples.text: 我能夠自己煮飯，可是不如我媽媽煮得好吃。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ nénggòu zìjǐ zhǔ fàn, kěshì bùrú wǒ māmā zhǔ dé hǎochī.
drill_examples.translation_en: I can cook by myself, but not as well as my mom.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 혼자서 밥을 할 수 있지만, 엄마가 하는 것만큼 맛있지는 않아요.
