incorrect_examples.error: Cannot use 能夠 for permission. Use 可以 instead.

transformation_drills: Change '我會說中文。' to use 能夠.
Change '他能夠游泳。' to a negative sentence.
production_drills: Tell your friend about a skill you have using 能夠.
Ask your classmate if they are able to come to the party.
common_error_patterns: Using 能夠 for simple physical abilities
Using 能夠 for permission

repair_feedback.word_order: Remember: 能夠 + Verb. Do not put the object before the verb.
repair_feedback.politeness: Using 能夠 is formal. In casual speech, 會 or 可以 is more common.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 能夠 for abilities that require effort or conditions, not for simple actions.

values.meaning_en: not as good as; would rather

explanation_en: Use this pattern to say a body part hurts. Place the body part before 痛.
title: Saying a Body Part Hurts
meaning: Body part hurts
when_to_use: When describing physical pain
When telling a doctor or friend where it hurts
when_not_to_use: For emotional pain
For general discomfort without a specific body part
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, people often say 頭痛, 肚子痛, 喉嚨痛. You can add 很 before 痛 to emphasize severity.
word_order_notes: Body part always comes before 痛, like 頭 + 痛 = 頭痛.
english_contrast: In English, you say 'My head hurts' or 'I have a headache'. In Chinese, just say 頭痛 without 'my'.
korean_contrast: In Korean, you say '머리가 아파요' (head + subject marker + hurts). Chinese is simpler: just 頭痛.

correct_examples.text: 我頭痛，從早上到現在一直很痛。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ tóu tòng, cóng zǎoshang dào xiànzài yīzhí hěn tòng.
correct_examples.translation_en: I have a headache; it has been hurting since this morning.

correct_examples.text: 他肚子痛，已經痛了兩個小時。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā dùzi tòng, yǐjīng tòng le liǎng ge xiǎoshí.
correct_examples.translation_en: He has a stomachache; it has been hurting for two hours.

correct_examples.text: 她喉嚨痛，還有咳嗽，所以想去看醫生。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā hóulóng tòng, hái yǒu késòu, suǒyǐ xiǎng qù kàn yīshēng.
correct_examples.translation_en: She has a sore throat and a cough, so she wants to see a doctor.

correct_examples.text: 我鼻子過敏，一直流鼻水，很不舒服。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ bízi guòmǐn, yīzhí liú bíshuǐ, hěn bù shūfu.
correct_examples.translation_en: I have a nose allergy; my nose keeps running, and it's very uncomfortable.

correct_examples.text: 他牙齒痛，從昨天開始到現在，越來越嚴重。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā yáchǐ tòng, cóng zuótiān kāishǐ dào xiànzài, yuè lái yuè yánzhòng.
correct_examples.translation_en: He has a toothache; it started yesterday and has been getting worse.

correct_examples.text: 我腳受傷了，現在很痛，需要休息。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiǎo shòushāng le, xiànzài hěn tòng, xūyào xiūxi.
correct_examples.translation_en: I injured my foot; it hurts a lot now, and I need to rest.

correct_examples.text: 她手痠，寫字寫了三個小時，受不了了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā shǒu suān, xiězì xiě le sān ge xiǎoshí, shòu bù liǎo le.
correct_examples.translation_en: Her hand is sore; she has been writing for three hours and can't stand it anymore.

correct_examples.text: 我頭暈，從下午開始到現在，一直很不舒服。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ tóuyūn, cóng xiàwǔ kāishǐ dào xiànzài, yīzhí hěn bù shūfu.
correct_examples.translation_en: I feel dizzy; it started in the afternoon and has been very uncomfortable.

drill_examples.text: 我頭痛，從早上到現在一直很痛。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ tóu tòng, cóng zǎoshang dào xiànzài yīzhí hěn tòng.
drill_examples.translation_en: I have a headache; it has been hurting since this morning.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 머리가 아파요. 아침부터 지금까지 계속 아파요.

drill_examples.text: 他肚子痛，已經痛了兩個小時。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā dùzi tòng, yǐjīng tòng le liǎng ge xiǎoshí.
drill_examples.translation_en: He has a stomachache; it has been hurting for two hours.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 배가 아파요. 벌써 두 시간 동안 아팠어요.

drill_examples.text: 她喉嚨痛，還有咳嗽，所以想去看醫生。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā hóulóng tòng, hái yǒu késòu, suǒyǐ xiǎng qù kàn yīshēng.
drill_examples.translation_en: She has a sore throat and a cough, so she wants to see a doctor.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그녀는 목이 아프고 기침도 해서 의사를 만나러 가고 싶어해요.

drill_examples.text: 我鼻子過敏，一直流鼻水，很不舒服。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ bízi guòmǐn, yīzhí liú bíshuǐ, hěn bù shūfu.
drill_examples.translation_en: I have a nose allergy; my nose keeps running, and it's very uncomfortable.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 코 알레르기가 있어서 계속 콧물이 나고, 많이 불편해요.

drill_examples.text: 他牙齒痛，從昨天開始到現在，越來越嚴重。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā yáchǐ tòng, cóng zuótiān kāishǐ dào xiànzài, yuè lái yuè yánzhòng.
drill_examples.translation_en: He has a toothache; it started yesterday and has been getting worse.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 이가 아파요. 어제부터 시작해서 점점 더 심해지고 있어요.

drill_examples.text: 我腳受傷了，現在很痛，需要休息。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiǎo shòushāng le, xiànzài hěn tòng, xūyào xiūxi.
drill_examples.translation_en: I injured my foot; it hurts a lot now, and I need to rest.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 발을 다쳤어요. 지금 많이 아파서 쉬어야 해요.

drill_examples.text: 她手痠，寫字寫了三個小時，受不了了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā shǒu suān, xiězì xiě le sān ge xiǎoshí, shòu bù liǎo le.
drill_examples.translation_en: Her hand is sore; she has been writing for three hours and can't stand it anymore.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그녀는 손이 저려요. 세 시간 동안 글을 써서 더 이상 참을 수 없어요.

drill_examples.text: 我頭暈，從下午開始到現在，一直很不舒服。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ tóuyūn, cóng xiàwǔ kāishǐ dào xiànzài, yīzhí hěn bù shūfu.
drill_examples.translation_en: I feel dizzy; it started in the afternoon and has been very uncomfortable.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 어지러워요. 오후부터 지금까지 계속 많이 불편해요.

negative_examples.error: Word order is wrong. Body part must come before 痛.

incorrect_examples.error: You don't need 我. Just say 頭痛.

transformation_drills: Change 頭痛 to 肚子痛
Change 喉嚨痛 to 牙齒痛
production_drills: Say 'My stomach hurts' in Chinese
Say 'My back hurts' in Chinese
common_error_patterns: Putting 痛 before the body part
Adding 我 unnecessarily

repair_feedback.word_order: Put the body part first, then 痛.
repair_feedback.politeness: This pattern is neutral and polite.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use body part words like 頭, 肚子, 喉嚨.

explanation_en: Use 有點 to say you have a mild symptom. It softens the statement.
title: Expressing Mild Symptoms
meaning: A little bit of symptom
when_to_use: When symptoms are not severe
When you want to sound polite or modest
when_not_to_use: For severe or serious symptoms
For positive feelings
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 有點 is common in daily conversation. For example, 有點咳嗽 means 'a little cough'.
word_order_notes: 有點 comes before the symptom word, like 有點 + 頭痛.
english_contrast: In English, you say 'I have a bit of a headache'. In Chinese, say 有點頭痛.
korean_contrast: In Korean, you say '머리가 조금 아파요' (head + a little + hurts). Chinese uses 有點 + symptom.

correct_examples.text: 我今天有點頭痛，所以想早點休息。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ jīntiān yǒudiǎn tóutòng, suǒyǐ xiǎng zǎodiǎn xiūxí.
correct_examples.translation_en: I have a bit of a headache today, so I want to rest early.

correct_examples.text: 他有點喉嚨痛，可能是感冒了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā yǒudiǎn hóulóng tòng, kěnéng shì gǎnmào le.
correct_examples.translation_en: He has a bit of a sore throat; maybe he caught a cold.

correct_examples.text: 我昨天開始有點咳嗽，到現在還是不舒服。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ zuótiān kāishǐ yǒudiǎn késòu, dào xiànzài háishì bù shūfú.
correct_examples.translation_en: I started coughing a little yesterday, and I still feel unwell now.

correct_examples.text: 她有點肚子痛，已經不舒服一個小時了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā yǒudiǎn dùzi tòng, yǐjīng bù shūfú yī ge xiǎoshí le.
correct_examples.translation_en: She has a bit of a stomachache and has been feeling unwell for an hour.

correct_examples.text: 我早上起床的時候有點頭暈，現在好一點了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ zǎoshang qǐchuáng de shíhou yǒudiǎn tóuyūn, xiànzài hǎo yīdiǎn le.
correct_examples.translation_en: I felt a bit dizzy when I got up this morning, but now I'm a little better.

correct_examples.text: 他有點發燒，所以今天沒有去上班。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā yǒudiǎn fāshāo, suǒyǐ jīntiān méiyǒu qù shàngbān.
correct_examples.translation_en: He has a slight fever, so he didn't go to work today.

correct_examples.text: 我喉嚨有點癢，一直想咳嗽。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ hóulóng yǒudiǎn yǎng, yīzhí xiǎng késòu.
correct_examples.translation_en: My throat is a bit itchy, and I keep wanting to cough.

correct_examples.text: 她鼻子有點不舒服，一直流鼻水。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā bízi yǒudiǎn bù shūfú, yīzhí liú bíshuǐ.
correct_examples.translation_en: Her nose feels a bit uncomfortable, and she keeps having a runny nose.

drill_examples.text: 我今天有點頭痛，所以想早點休息。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ jīntiān yǒudiǎn tóutòng, suǒyǐ xiǎng zǎodiǎn xiūxí.
drill_examples.translation_en: I have a bit of a headache today, so I want to rest early.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 오늘 머리가 조금 아파서 일찍 쉬고 싶어요.

drill_examples.text: 他有點喉嚨痛，可能是感冒了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā yǒudiǎn hóulóng tòng, kěnéng shì gǎnmào le.
drill_examples.translation_en: He has a bit of a sore throat; maybe he caught a cold.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 목이 조금 아파요. 아마 감기에 걸린 것 같아요.

drill_examples.text: 我昨天開始有點咳嗽，到現在還是不舒服。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ zuótiān kāishǐ yǒudiǎn késòu, dào xiànzài háishì bù shūfú.
drill_examples.translation_en: I started coughing a little yesterday, and I still feel unwell now.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 어제부터 기침이 조금 나기 시작했는데, 지금도 여전히 편하지 않아요.

drill_examples.text: 她有點肚子痛，已經不舒服一個小時了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā yǒudiǎn dùzi tòng, yǐjīng bù shūfú yī ge xiǎoshí le.
drill_examples.translation_en: She has a bit of a stomachache and has been feeling unwell for an hour.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그녀는 배가 조금 아파요. 벌써 한 시간 동안 편하지 않아요.

drill_examples.text: 我早上起床的時候有點頭暈，現在好一點了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ zǎoshang qǐchuáng de shíhou yǒudiǎn tóuyūn, xiànzài hǎo yīdiǎn le.
drill_examples.translation_en: I felt a bit dizzy when I got up this morning, but now I'm a little better.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 아침에 일어날 때 어지러움이 조금 있었는데, 지금은 좀 나아졌어요.

drill_examples.text: 他有點發燒，所以今天沒有去上班。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā yǒudiǎn fāshāo, suǒyǐ jīntiān méiyǒu qù shàngbān.
drill_examples.translation_en: He has a slight fever, so he didn't go to work today.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 열이 조금 있어서 오늘 출근하지 않았어요.

drill_examples.text: 我喉嚨有點癢，一直想咳嗽。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ hóulóng yǒudiǎn yǎng, yīzhí xiǎng késòu.
drill_examples.translation_en: My throat is a bit itchy, and I keep wanting to cough.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 목이 조금 간지러워서 계속 기침이 나요.

drill_examples.text: 她鼻子有點不舒服，一直流鼻水。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā bízi yǒudiǎn bù shūfú, yīzhí liú bíshuǐ.
drill_examples.translation_en: Her nose feels a bit uncomfortable, and she keeps having a runny nose.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그녀는 코가 조금 불편해서 계속 콧물이 나요.

negative_examples.error: 有點 must come before the symptom.

incorrect_examples.error: Don't use 很 with 有點. 有點 already means 'a little'.

transformation_drills: Change 有點頭痛 to 有點肚子痛
Change 有點咳嗽 to 有點流鼻水
production_drills: Say 'I have a little fever' in Chinese
Say 'I feel a bit dizzy' in Chinese
common_error_patterns: Putting 有點 after the symptom
Adding 很 after 有點

repair_feedback.word_order: Put 有點 before the symptom.
repair_feedback.politeness: Using 有點 makes you sound polite and not complaining too much.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use symptom words like 頭痛, 咳嗽, 發燒.

pattern: 從 + 時間 + 開始 + 症狀 + 到現在
explanation_en: Use this pattern to say when a symptom started and that it continues until now.
title: Saying How Long a Symptom Has Lasted
meaning: Symptom started at time and continues to now
when_to_use: When telling a doctor when symptoms began
When describing ongoing symptoms
when_not_to_use: For symptoms that have already ended
Without specifying a start time
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, you can say 從昨天開始頭痛到現在 to mean 'headache from yesterday until now'.
word_order_notes: Start with 從, then time, then 開始, then symptom, then 到現在.
english_contrast: In English, you say 'I've had a headache since yesterday'. In Chinese, say 從昨天開始頭痛到現在.
korean_contrast: In Korean, you say '어제부터 지금까지 머리가 아파요' (from yesterday to now head hurts). Chinese structure is similar but uses 開始.

correct_examples.text: 我從昨天開始頭痛到現在。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ cóng zuótiān kāishǐ tóutòng dào xiànzài.
correct_examples.translation_en: I have had a headache from yesterday until now.

correct_examples.text: 他從早上開始喉嚨痛，到現在還沒好。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā cóng zǎoshang kāishǐ hóulóng tòng, dào xiànzài hái méi hǎo.
correct_examples.translation_en: He has had a sore throat since this morning and it still hasn't gotten better.

correct_examples.text: 她從前天開始咳嗽，到現在已經三天了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā cóng qiántiān kāishǐ késòu, dào xiànzài yǐjīng sān tiān le.
correct_examples.translation_en: She has been coughing since the day before yesterday, and it has been three days now.

correct_examples.text: 我從昨天晚上開始發燒，到現在還在發燒。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ cóng zuótiān wǎnshang kāishǐ fāshāo, dào xiànzài hái zài fāshāo.
correct_examples.translation_en: I have had a fever since last night and I still have it now.

correct_examples.text: 他從三小時前開始肚子痛，到現在越來越嚴重。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā cóng sān xiǎoshí qián kāishǐ dùzi tòng, dào xiànzài yuè lái yuè yánzhòng.
correct_examples.translation_en: He has had a stomachache since three hours ago, and it is getting more and more serious now.

correct_examples.text: 我從昨天開始流鼻水，到現在一直沒停。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ cóng zuótiān kāishǐ liú bíshuǐ, dào xiànzài yīzhí méi tíng.
correct_examples.translation_en: I have had a runny nose since yesterday, and it hasn't stopped until now.

correct_examples.text: 她從今天早上開始頭暈，到現在還是不舒服。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā cóng jīntiān zǎoshang kāishǐ tóuyūn, dào xiànzài háishì bù shūfú.
correct_examples.translation_en: She has felt dizzy since this morning and still feels unwell now.

correct_examples.text: 我從昨天開始想吐，到現在已經受不了了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ cóng zuótiān kāishǐ xiǎng tù, dào xiànzài yǐjīng shòu bù liǎo le.
correct_examples.translation_en: I have felt nauseous since yesterday, and I can't stand it anymore now.

drill_examples.text: 我從昨天開始頭痛到現在。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ cóng zuótiān kāishǐ tóutòng dào xiànzài.
drill_examples.translation_en: I have had a headache from yesterday until now.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 어제부터 지금까지 두통이 있습니다.

drill_examples.text: 他從早上開始喉嚨痛，到現在還沒好。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā cóng zǎoshang kāishǐ hóulóng tòng, dào xiànzài hái méi hǎo.
drill_examples.translation_en: He has had a sore throat since this morning and it still hasn't gotten better.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 아침부터 목이 아팠는데, 지금까지 아직 낫지 않았어요.

drill_examples.text: 她從前天開始咳嗽，到現在已經三天了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā cóng qiántiān kāishǐ késòu, dào xiànzài yǐjīng sān tiān le.
drill_examples.translation_en: She has been coughing since the day before yesterday, and it has been three days now.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그녀는 그저께부터 기침을 했는데, 지금까지 벌써 사흘이 되었어요.

drill_examples.text: 我從昨天晚上開始發燒，到現在還在發燒。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ cóng zuótiān wǎnshang kāishǐ fāshāo, dào xiànzài hái zài fāshāo.
drill_examples.translation_en: I have had a fever since last night and I still have it now.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 어젯밤부터 열이 났는데, 지금까지 계속 열이 있어요.

drill_examples.text: 他從三小時前開始肚子痛，到現在越來越嚴重。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā cóng sān xiǎoshí qián kāishǐ dùzi tòng, dào xiànzài yuè lái yuè yánzhòng.
drill_examples.translation_en: He has had a stomachache since three hours ago, and it is getting more and more serious now.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 세 시간 전부터 배가 아팠는데, 지금까지 점점 더 심해지고 있어요.

drill_examples.text: 我從昨天開始流鼻水，到現在一直沒停。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ cóng zuótiān kāishǐ liú bíshuǐ, dào xiànzài yīzhí méi tíng.
drill_examples.translation_en: I have had a runny nose since yesterday, and it hasn't stopped until now.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 어제부터 콧물이 났는데, 지금까지 계속 멈추지 않았어요.

drill_examples.text: 她從今天早上開始頭暈，到現在還是不舒服。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā cóng jīntiān zǎoshang kāishǐ tóuyūn, dào xiànzài háishì bù shūfú.
drill_examples.translation_en: She has felt dizzy since this morning and still feels unwell now.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그녀는 오늘 아침부터 어지러웠는데, 지금까지 여전히 편하지 않아요.

drill_examples.text: 我從昨天開始想吐，到現在已經受不了了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ cóng zuótiān kāishǐ xiǎng tù, dào xiànzài yǐjīng shòu bù liǎo le.
drill_examples.translation_en: I have felt nauseous since yesterday, and I can't stand it anymore now.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 어제부터 토할 것 같았는데, 지금까지 이미 참을 수 없게 되었어요.

negative_examples.text: 頭痛從昨天開始到現在
negative_examples.error: Symptom should come after 開始, not at the beginning.

incorrect_examples.text: 從昨天頭痛開始到現在
incorrect_examples.error: 時間 must come right after 從, not the symptom.

transformation_drills: Change 從昨天開始頭痛到現在 to 從早上開始咳嗽到現在
Change 從前天開始發燒到現在 to 從今天開始肚子痛到現在
production_drills: Say 'My throat has hurt since this morning' in Chinese
Say 'I've had a fever since yesterday' in Chinese
common_error_patterns: Putting symptom before 從
Omitting 開始 or 到現在

repair_feedback.word_order: Follow the order: 從 + time + 開始 + symptom + 到現在.
repair_feedback.politeness: This pattern is clear and polite for medical contexts.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use time words like 昨天, 早上, 前天.

pattern: 吃 + (量詞) + 藥 + (時間/頻率)
explanation_en: Use this pattern to talk about taking medicine, specifying the type, dosage, and frequency.
title: Taking Medicine (吃藥)
meaning: To take medicine
when_to_use: When describing medication intake
When asking about dosage or frequency
when_not_to_use: When talking about applying ointment (use 擦藥膏 instead)
When referring to injections (use 打針)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 藥師 (pharmacist) is commonly used at 藥房 (pharmacy). You can ask 藥師 for advice on 成藥 (over-the-counter medicine).
word_order_notes: Time/frequency usually comes after the object, e.g., 吃藥一天三次.
english_contrast: English says 'take medicine three times a day'; Chinese order is 'eat medicine one day three times'.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '약을 먹다' (eat medicine) similarly, but frequency often precedes the verb.

correct_examples.text: 我喉嚨痛，想去藥房買藥。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ hóulóng tòng, xiǎng qù yàofáng mǎi yào.
correct_examples.translation_en: I have a sore throat and want to go to the pharmacy to buy medicine.

correct_examples.text: 醫生說這個藥一天吃三次，飯後吃。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yīshēng shuō zhège yào yī tiān chī sān cì, fàn hòu chī.
correct_examples.translation_en: The doctor said to take this medicine three times a day, after meals.

correct_examples.text: 請問藥師，這個藥水有副作用嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn yàoshī, zhège yàoshuǐ yǒu fùzuòyòng ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, pharmacist, does this liquid medicine have side effects?

correct_examples.text: 我對這個藥過敏，不能吃。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ duì zhège yào guòmǐn, bù néng chī.
correct_examples.translation_en: I am allergic to this medicine, so I can't take it.

correct_examples.text: 護士說打針前要先量體溫。
correct_examples.pinyin: Hùshì shuō dǎ zhēn qián yào xiān liáng tǐwēn.
correct_examples.translation_en: The nurse said you need to take your temperature before getting an injection.

correct_examples.text: 這個藥膏一天擦兩次，早上和睡前。
correct_examples.pinyin: Zhège yàogāo yī tiān cā liǎng cì, zǎoshang hé shuì qián.
correct_examples.translation_en: Apply this ointment twice a day, in the morning and before bed.

correct_examples.text: 藥師說這個藥丸要配水吃，不能咬。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yàoshī shuō zhège yàowán yào pèi shuǐ chī, bù néng yǎo.
correct_examples.translation_en: The pharmacist said to take this pill with water and not to chew it.

correct_examples.text: 我頭痛，去診所看醫生，他給我開了藥。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ tóutòng, qù zhěnsuǒ kàn yīshēng, tā gěi wǒ kāi le yào.
correct_examples.translation_en: I had a headache, went to the clinic to see a doctor, and he prescribed me medicine.

drill_examples.text: 我喉嚨痛，想去藥房買藥。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ hóulóng tòng, xiǎng qù yàofáng mǎi yào.
drill_examples.translation_en: I have a sore throat and want to go to the pharmacy to buy medicine.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 목이 아파서 약국에 가서 약을 사고 싶어요.

drill_examples.text: 醫生說這個藥一天吃三次，飯後吃。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yīshēng shuō zhège yào yī tiān chī sān cì, fàn hòu chī.
drill_examples.translation_en: The doctor said to take this medicine three times a day, after meals.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 의사 선생님이 이 약은 하루에 세 번, 식후에 드시라고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 請問藥師，這個藥水有副作用嗎？
drill_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn yàoshī, zhège yàoshuǐ yǒu fùzuòyòng ma?
drill_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, pharmacist, does this liquid medicine have side effects?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 약사님, 이 물약에 부작용이 있나요?

drill_examples.text: 我對這個藥過敏，不能吃。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ duì zhège yào guòmǐn, bù néng chī.
drill_examples.translation_en: I am allergic to this medicine, so I can't take it.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 이 약에 알레르기가 있어서 먹을 수 없어요.

drill_examples.text: 護士說打針前要先量體溫。
drill_examples.pinyin: Hùshì shuō dǎ zhēn qián yào xiān liáng tǐwēn.
drill_examples.translation_en: The nurse said you need to take your temperature before getting an injection.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 간호사가 주사 맞기 전에 먼저 체온을 재야 한다고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 這個藥膏一天擦兩次，早上和睡前。
drill_examples.pinyin: Zhège yàogāo yī tiān cā liǎng cì, zǎoshang hé shuì qián.
drill_examples.translation_en: Apply this ointment twice a day, in the morning and before bed.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 이 연고는 하루에 두 번, 아침과 자기 전에 바르세요.

drill_examples.text: 藥師說這個藥丸要配水吃，不能咬。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yàoshī shuō zhège yàowán yào pèi shuǐ chī, bù néng yǎo.
drill_examples.translation_en: The pharmacist said to take this pill with water and not to chew it.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 약사가 이 알약은 물과 함께 드시고 씹지 마세요라고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 我頭痛，去診所看醫生，他給我開了藥。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ tóutòng, qù zhěnsuǒ kàn yīshēng, tā gěi wǒ kāi le yào.
drill_examples.translation_en: I had a headache, went to the clinic to see a doctor, and he prescribed me medicine.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 두통이 있어서 병원에 가서 의사를 봤는데, 약을 처방해 주셨어요.

negative_examples.error: Incorrect word order; should be 我一天吃一次藥水.

incorrect_examples.error: Missing time frame; should specify 一天兩次.

transformation_drills: Change 我吃藥一天三次 to 我一天吃三次藥.
Change 他吃藥水一天兩次 to 他一天吃兩次藥水.
production_drills: Ask your partner: 你一天吃幾次藥？
Tell the pharmacist: 我一天吃三次藥丸。
common_error_patterns: Omitting time frame
Using wrong measure word for liquid medicine (should be 次, not 杯)

repair_feedback.word_order: Put time/frequency after the object, e.g., 吃藥一天三次.
repair_feedback.politeness: Use 請問 when asking the pharmacist.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 藥水 for liquid medicine, 藥丸 for pills.

pattern: 每天 + 次數 + 動詞 + 藥
explanation_en: Use this pattern to express how many times per day you do something, especially taking medicine.
title: Frequency per Day (每天...次)
meaning: How many times a day
when_to_use: When stating daily medication frequency
When asking about daily habits
when_not_to_use: When talking about total dosage (use 總共 instead)
When referring to weekly frequency (use 每星期)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, doctors often say 一天三次, 飯後 (three times a day, after meals). 飯前 means before meals, 睡前 means before sleep.
word_order_notes: 每天 comes first, then the number of times, then the verb and object.
english_contrast: English says 'take medicine three times a day'; Chinese says 'every day three times take medicine'.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '하루에 세 번' (three times a day) similarly placed before the verb.

correct_examples.text: 醫生說我每天要吃三次藥。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yīshēng shuō wǒ měitiān yào chī sān cì yào.
correct_examples.translation_en: The doctor said I need to take medicine three times a day.

correct_examples.text: 藥師說這個藥水每天喝兩次，飯後喝。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yàoshī shuō zhège yàoshuǐ měitiān hē liǎng cì, fàn hòu hē.
correct_examples.translation_en: The pharmacist said to drink this liquid medicine twice a day, after meals.

correct_examples.text: 我對這個藥過敏，所以每天只能吃一次。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ duì zhège yào guòmǐn, suǒyǐ měitiān zhǐ néng chī yī cì.
correct_examples.translation_en: I am allergic to this medicine, so I can only take it once a day.

correct_examples.text: 護士說這個藥膏每天要擦三次，早上、中午和晚上。
correct_examples.pinyin: Hùshì shuō zhège yàogāo měitiān yào cā sān cì, zǎoshang, zhōngwǔ hé wǎnshang.
correct_examples.translation_en: The nurse said to apply this ointment three times a day: morning, noon, and evening.

correct_examples.text: 醫生說這個藥丸每天吃四次，飯前吃。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yīshēng shuō zhège yàowán měitiān chī sì cì, fàn qián chī.
correct_examples.translation_en: The doctor said to take this pill four times a day, before meals.

correct_examples.text: 藥師說這個藥水每天喝一次，睡前喝。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yàoshī shuō zhège yàoshuǐ měitiān hē yī cì, shuì qián hē.
correct_examples.translation_en: The pharmacist said to drink this liquid medicine once a day, before bed.

correct_examples.text: 因為我頭痛，所以醫生說每天吃兩次藥就可以了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yīnwèi wǒ tóutòng, suǒyǐ yīshēng shuō měitiān chī liǎng cì yào jiù kěyǐ le.
correct_examples.translation_en: Because I have a headache, the doctor said taking medicine twice a day is enough.

correct_examples.text: 這個藥的副作用是會想睡覺，所以每天只能吃一次。
correct_examples.pinyin: Zhège yào de fùzuòyòng shì huì xiǎng shuìjiào, suǒyǐ měitiān zhǐ néng chī yī cì.
correct_examples.translation_en: The side effect of this medicine is drowsiness, so you can only take it once a day.

drill_examples.text: 醫生說我每天要吃三次藥。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yīshēng shuō wǒ měitiān yào chī sān cì yào.
drill_examples.translation_en: The doctor said I need to take medicine three times a day.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 의사 선생님이 제가 하루에 세 번 약을 먹어야 한다고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 藥師說這個藥水每天喝兩次，飯後喝。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yàoshī shuō zhège yàoshuǐ měitiān hē liǎng cì, fàn hòu hē.
drill_examples.translation_en: The pharmacist said to drink this liquid medicine twice a day, after meals.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 약사 선생님이 이 물약을 하루에 두 번, 식후에 마시라고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 我對這個藥過敏，所以每天只能吃一次。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ duì zhège yào guòmǐn, suǒyǐ měitiān zhǐ néng chī yī cì.
drill_examples.translation_en: I am allergic to this medicine, so I can only take it once a day.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 이 약에 알레르기가 있어서 하루에 한 번만 먹을 수 있어요.

drill_examples.text: 護士說這個藥膏每天要擦三次，早上、中午和晚上。
drill_examples.pinyin: Hùshì shuō zhège yàogāo měitiān yào cā sān cì, zǎoshang, zhōngwǔ hé wǎnshang.
drill_examples.translation_en: The nurse said to apply this ointment three times a day: morning, noon, and evening.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 간호사 선생님이 이 연고를 하루에 세 번, 아침, 점심, 저녁에 바르라고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 醫生說這個藥丸每天吃四次，飯前吃。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yīshēng shuō zhège yàowán měitiān chī sì cì, fàn qián chī.
drill_examples.translation_en: The doctor said to take this pill four times a day, before meals.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 의사 선생님이 이 알약을 하루에 네 번, 식전에 먹으라고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 藥師說這個藥水每天喝一次，睡前喝。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yàoshī shuō zhège yàoshuǐ měitiān hē yī cì, shuì qián hē.
drill_examples.translation_en: The pharmacist said to drink this liquid medicine once a day, before bed.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 약사 선생님이 이 물약을 하루에 한 번, 자기 전에 마시라고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 因為我頭痛，所以醫生說每天吃兩次藥就可以了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yīnwèi wǒ tóutòng, suǒyǐ yīshēng shuō měitiān chī liǎng cì yào jiù kěyǐ le.
drill_examples.translation_en: Because I have a headache, the doctor said taking medicine twice a day is enough.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 제가 두통이 있어서 의사 선생님이 하루에 두 번 약을 먹으면 된다고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 這個藥的副作用是會想睡覺，所以每天只能吃一次。
drill_examples.pinyin: Zhège yào de fùzuòyòng shì huì xiǎng shuìjiào, suǒyǐ měitiān zhǐ néng chī yī cì.
drill_examples.translation_en: The side effect of this medicine is drowsiness, so you can only take it once a day.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 이 약의 부작용은 졸음이 오는 거라서 하루에 한 번만 먹을 수 있어요.

negative_examples.error: 每天 should come before the verb phrase.

incorrect_examples.error: Missing the number of times; should specify 每天三次.

transformation_drills: Change 我每天吃藥三次 to 我一天吃三次藥.
Change 他每天吃藥水兩次 to 他一天吃兩次藥水.
production_drills: Ask: 你每天吃幾次藥？
Answer: 我每天吃兩次藥丸。
common_error_patterns: Forgetting to specify the number of times
Using 每天 with 一次 incorrectly placed

repair_feedback.word_order: Place 每天 at the beginning of the sentence.
repair_feedback.politeness: Use 請問 when asking about medication.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 次 for times, 藥丸 for pills, 藥水 for liquid.

pattern: 可以 + 動詞 + 嗎？ / 不可以 + 動詞
explanation_en: Use 可以 to ask for permission or possibility, and 不可以 to deny permission, especially in medical contexts.
title: Permission and Possibility (可以/不可以)
meaning: Can / Cannot
when_to_use: When asking if you can take medicine
When asking if something is allowed
When denying permission
when_not_to_use: When talking about ability (use 會 instead)
When making polite requests (use 請 or 不好意思)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, at a 診所 (clinic), you can ask 醫生: 我可以吃這個藥嗎？ (Can I take this medicine?). 不可以 is direct; use 不能 for softer negation.
word_order_notes: 可以 comes before the verb; 嗎 at the end for questions.
english_contrast: English uses 'can' similarly, but Chinese places 可以 before the verb.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '~을/를 먹어도 돼요?' (can I eat?) similarly structured.

correct_examples.text: 請問，這裡可以買成藥嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, zhèlǐ kěyǐ mǎi chéngyào ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, can I buy over-the-counter medicine here?

correct_examples.text: 醫生說不可以吃藥前喝酒。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yīshēng shuō bù kěyǐ chī yào qián hē jiǔ.
correct_examples.translation_en: The doctor said you cannot drink alcohol before taking medicine.

correct_examples.text: 我對這個藥過敏，可以換一種嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ duì zhège yào guòmǐn, kěyǐ huàn yī zhǒng ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: I am allergic to this medicine; can I switch to another one?

correct_examples.text: 藥師說這個藥水不可以一次喝太多。
correct_examples.pinyin: Yàoshī shuō zhège yàoshuǐ bù kěyǐ yīcì hē tài duō.
correct_examples.translation_en: The pharmacist said you cannot drink too much of this liquid medicine at once.

correct_examples.text: 請問，可以在這裡量體溫嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, kěyǐ zài zhèlǐ liáng tǐwēn ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, can I take my temperature here?

correct_examples.text: 護士說現在不可以進去，醫生在忙。
correct_examples.pinyin: Hùshì shuō xiànzài bù kěyǐ jìnqù, yīshēng zài máng.
correct_examples.translation_en: The nurse said you cannot go in now; the doctor is busy.

correct_examples.text: 這個藥膏可以每天用，但是不可以太多。
correct_examples.pinyin: Zhège yàogāo kěyǐ měitiān yòng, dànshì bù kěyǐ tài duō.
correct_examples.translation_en: This ointment can be used every day, but not too much.

correct_examples.text: 請問，我可以先掛號再看醫生嗎？
correct_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, wǒ kěyǐ xiān guàhào zài kàn yīshēng ma?
correct_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, can I register first and then see the doctor?

drill_examples.text: 請問，這裡可以買成藥嗎？
drill_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, zhèlǐ kěyǐ mǎi chéngyào ma?
drill_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, can I buy over-the-counter medicine here?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 실례합니다, 여기서 일반의약품을 살 수 있나요?

drill_examples.text: 醫生說不可以吃藥前喝酒。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yīshēng shuō bù kěyǐ chī yào qián hē jiǔ.
drill_examples.translation_en: The doctor said you cannot drink alcohol before taking medicine.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 의사 선생님이 약 먹기 전에 술을 마시면 안 된다고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 我對這個藥過敏，可以換一種嗎？
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ duì zhège yào guòmǐn, kěyǐ huàn yī zhǒng ma?
drill_examples.translation_en: I am allergic to this medicine; can I switch to another one?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 이 약에 알레르기가 있는데, 다른 걸로 바꿀 수 있나요?

drill_examples.text: 藥師說這個藥水不可以一次喝太多。
drill_examples.pinyin: Yàoshī shuō zhège yàoshuǐ bù kěyǐ yīcì hē tài duō.
drill_examples.translation_en: The pharmacist said you cannot drink too much of this liquid medicine at once.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 약사 선생님이 이 물약을 한 번에 너무 많이 마시면 안 된다고 했어요.

drill_examples.text: 請問，可以在這裡量體溫嗎？
drill_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, kěyǐ zài zhèlǐ liáng tǐwēn ma?
drill_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, can I take my temperature here?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 실례합니다, 여기서 체온을 쟐 수 있나요?

drill_examples.text: 護士說現在不可以進去，醫生在忙。
drill_examples.pinyin: Hùshì shuō xiànzài bù kěyǐ jìnqù, yīshēng zài máng.
drill_examples.translation_en: The nurse said you cannot go in now; the doctor is busy.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 간호사 선생님이 지금은 들어가면 안 된다고 했어요. 의사 선생님이 바쁘세요.

drill_examples.text: 這個藥膏可以每天用，但是不可以太多。
drill_examples.pinyin: Zhège yàogāo kěyǐ měitiān yòng, dànshì bù kěyǐ tài duō.
drill_examples.translation_en: This ointment can be used every day, but not too much.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 이 연고는 매일 사용할 수 있지만, 너무 많이 바르면 안 돼요.

drill_examples.text: 請問，我可以先掛號再看醫生嗎？
drill_examples.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, wǒ kěyǐ xiān guàhào zài kàn yīshēng ma?
drill_examples.translation_en: Excuse me, can I register first and then see the doctor?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 실례합니다, 먼저 접수하고 의사 선생님을 봐도 되나요?

negative_examples.error: Correct, but avoid using 可以 alone as answer; use 可以 or 可以啊.

incorrect_examples.error: Should be 我不能吃藥 for inability; 不可以 implies permission denied.

transformation_drills: Change 我可以吃藥嗎？ to 我可以吃藥丸嗎？
Change 不可以吃藥 to 不能吃藥 for softer tone.
production_drills: Ask the doctor: 我可以吃這個藥嗎？
Tell the pharmacist: 我不可以吃藥水。
common_error_patterns: Confusing 不可以 (permission) with 不能 (ability)
Using 可以 for ability instead of 會

repair_feedback.word_order: Place 可以 before the verb.
repair_feedback.politeness: Use 請問 before asking permission.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 可以 for permission, 會 for ability.

explanation_en: Use '應該' to express that something should be done based on reason, expectation, or social norm. It is softer than '必須' and often used for advice.
title: 應該 for Should
meaning: Should / ought to
when_to_use: Giving advice or suggestions
Expressing what is expected or appropriate
Talking about obligations that are not strict
when_not_to_use: For strong obligations or rules (use 必須)
For personal desires (use 想)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, '應該' is common in polite advice. It can also mean 'probably' when used with uncertainty, e.g., '他應該會來'.
word_order_notes: Place '應該' before the main verb, after the subject. Negation: '不應該' (should not).
english_contrast: English uses 'should' similarly, but Chinese does not have a separate modal for 'ought to'.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '-아/어야 하다' for obligation, but '應該' is closer to '-는 것이 좋다' (it is good to).

correct_examples.text: 你應該早點睡覺。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi zǎo diǎn shuìjiào.
correct_examples.translation_en: You should go to bed earlier.

correct_examples.text: 我們必須遵守規定。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bìxū zūnshǒu guīdìng.
correct_examples.translation_en: We must follow the rules.

correct_examples.text: 你不需要買那麼多東西。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bù xūyào mǎi nàme duō dōngxī.
correct_examples.translation_en: You don't need to buy so many things.

correct_examples.text: 我建議你試試看這個。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiànyì nǐ shì shì kàn zhège.
correct_examples.translation_en: I suggest you give this a try.

correct_examples.text: 你最好帶一把雨傘。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ zuìhǎo dài yī bǎ yǔsǎn.
correct_examples.translation_en: You had better bring an umbrella.

correct_examples.text: 他不應該對客人那麼兇。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā bù yīnggāi duì kèrén nàme xiōng.
correct_examples.translation_en: He shouldn't be so mean to the customers.

correct_examples.text: 你得先吃完飯才能吃甜點。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ děi xiān chī wán fàn cáinéng chī tiándiǎn.
correct_examples.translation_en: You have to finish your meal first before you can have dessert.

correct_examples.text: 為了健康，你應該多運動。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wèile jiànkāng, nǐ yīnggāi duō yùndòng.
correct_examples.translation_en: For your health, you should exercise more.

drill_examples.text: 你應該早點睡覺。
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi zǎo diǎn shuìjiào.
drill_examples.translation_en: You should go to bed earlier.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 당신은 좀 더 일찍 자야 해요.

drill_examples.text: 我們必須遵守規定。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bìxū zūnshǒu guīdìng.
drill_examples.translation_en: We must follow the rules.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 우리는 규칙을 지켜야 해요.

drill_examples.text: 你不需要買那麼多東西。
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bù xūyào mǎi nàme duō dōngxī.
drill_examples.translation_en: You don't need to buy so many things.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 당신은 그렇게 많은 물건을 살 필요가 없어요.

drill_examples.text: 我建議你試試看這個。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiànyì nǐ shì shì kàn zhège.
drill_examples.translation_en: I suggest you give this a try.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 이걸 한번 시도해 보시길 권해요.

drill_examples.text: 你最好帶一把雨傘。
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ zuìhǎo dài yī bǎ yǔsǎn.
drill_examples.translation_en: You had better bring an umbrella.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 우산을 가져가는 것이 좋아요.

drill_examples.text: 他不應該對客人那麼兇。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā bù yīnggāi duì kèrén nàme xiōng.
drill_examples.translation_en: He shouldn't be so mean to the customers.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 손님에게 그렇게 무례하게 굴면 안 돼요.

drill_examples.text: 你得先吃完飯才能吃甜點。
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ děi xiān chī wán fàn cáinéng chī tiándiǎn.
drill_examples.translation_en: You have to finish your meal first before you can have dessert.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 디저트를 먹으려면 먼저 밥을 다 먹어야 해요.

drill_examples.text: 為了健康，你應該多運動。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wèile jiànkāng, nǐ yīnggāi duō yùndòng.
drill_examples.translation_en: For your health, you should exercise more.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 건강을 위해 운동을 더 많이 해야 해요.

negative_examples.error: Negation should be '不應該' before the verb, not after '應該'.

incorrect_examples.error: This is correct. No error here.

transformation_drills: Change '我們應該早點到' to a question.
production_drills: Give advice to a friend who is tired.
Say what you should do before an exam.
common_error_patterns: Placing '不' after '應該' instead of before
Using '應該' for strong rules

repair_feedback.politeness: Use '應該' for polite advice, not commands.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use '必須' for strong obligations.

explanation_en: Use '需要' to express that something is needed or necessary. It can also mean 'need to' as a verb. It is less strong than '必須' but more concrete than '應該'.
title: 需要 for Need
meaning: Need to / must (less strong)
when_to_use: Expressing necessity based on situation
Talking about requirements
Asking if something is needed
when_not_to_use: For moral obligation (use 應該)
For strict rules (use 必須)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, '需要' is common in service contexts, e.g., '你需要袋子嗎？' It can also be a noun meaning 'need'.
word_order_notes: Place '需要' before the main verb. Negation: '不需要' (don't need to).
english_contrast: English 'need to' is similar, but Chinese '需要' can also be used as a main verb (我需要水).
korean_contrast: Korean uses '필요하다' for need, but '需要' is more like '-아/어야 하다' in necessity.

correct_examples.text: 你需要先預約才能看醫生。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ xūyào xiān yùyuē cáinéng kàn yīshēng.
correct_examples.translation_en: You need to make an appointment first to see a doctor.

correct_examples.text: 我們必須遵守醫院的規定。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bìxū zūnshǒu yīyuàn de guīdìng.
correct_examples.translation_en: We must follow the hospital's rules.

correct_examples.text: 你應該要早點睡覺，不然明天會很累。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi yào zǎo diǎn shuìjiào, bùrán míngtiān huì hěn lèi.
correct_examples.translation_en: You should go to bed early, otherwise you'll be very tired tomorrow.

correct_examples.text: 我建議你帶雨傘，免得下雨。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiànyì nǐ dài yǔsǎn, miǎnde xiàyǔ.
correct_examples.translation_en: I suggest you bring an umbrella, so that you won't get caught in the rain.

correct_examples.text: 你不必買新的，舊的還可以穿。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bùbì mǎi xīn de, jiù de hái kěyǐ chuān.
correct_examples.translation_en: You don't need to buy a new one; the old one is still wearable.

correct_examples.text: 為了省錢，他寧願走路去公司。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wèile shěng qián, tā nìngyuàn zǒulù qù gōngsī.
correct_examples.translation_en: In order to save money, he would rather walk to the office.

correct_examples.text: 你必須在三天內退貨，否則不能退款。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū zài sān tiān nèi tuìhuò, fǒuzé bùnéng tuìkuǎn.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must return the item within three days, otherwise you can't get a refund.

correct_examples.text: 你不應該在圖書館裡大聲說話。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bù yīnggāi zài túshūguǎn lǐ dàshēng shuōhuà.
correct_examples.translation_en: You shouldn't speak loudly in the library.

drill_examples.text: 你需要先預約才能看醫生。
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ xūyào xiān yùyuē cáinéng kàn yīshēng.
drill_examples.translation_en: You need to make an appointment first to see a doctor.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 의사를 보려면 먼저 예약을 해야 합니다.

drill_examples.text: 我們必須遵守醫院的規定。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bìxū zūnshǒu yīyuàn de guīdìng.
drill_examples.translation_en: We must follow the hospital's rules.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 우리는 병원의 규정을 반드시 지켜야 합니다.

drill_examples.text: 你應該要早點睡覺，不然明天會很累。
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi yào zǎo diǎn shuìjiào, bùrán míngtiān huì hěn lèi.
drill_examples.translation_en: You should go to bed early, otherwise you'll be very tired tomorrow.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 일찍 자야 해요, 그렇지 않으면 내일 많이 피곤할 거예요.

drill_examples.text: 我建議你帶雨傘，免得下雨。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiànyì nǐ dài yǔsǎn, miǎnde xiàyǔ.
drill_examples.translation_en: I suggest you bring an umbrella, so that you won't get caught in the rain.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 우산을 가져가시는 게 좋겠어요, 비가 오지 않도록요.

drill_examples.text: 你不必買新的，舊的還可以穿。
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bùbì mǎi xīn de, jiù de hái kěyǐ chuān.
drill_examples.translation_en: You don't need to buy a new one; the old one is still wearable.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 새로 살 필요 없어요, 옛날 것도 아직 입을 수 있어요.

drill_examples.text: 為了省錢，他寧願走路去公司。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wèile shěng qián, tā nìngyuàn zǒulù qù gōngsī.
drill_examples.translation_en: In order to save money, he would rather walk to the office.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 돈을 아끼기 위해 그는 회사에 걸어가는 쪽을 택합니다.

drill_examples.text: 你必須在三天內退貨，否則不能退款。
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū zài sān tiān nèi tuìhuò, fǒuzé bùnéng tuìkuǎn.
drill_examples.translation_en: You must return the item within three days, otherwise you can't get a refund.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 3일 안에 반품해야 합니다. 그렇지 않으면 환불이 안 됩니다.

drill_examples.text: 你不應該在圖書館裡大聲說話。
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bù yīnggāi zài túshūguǎn lǐ dàshēng shuōhuà.
drill_examples.translation_en: You shouldn't speak loudly in the library.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 도서관에서 큰 소리로 말하면 안 됩니다.

negative_examples.error: Negation should be '不需要' before the verb, not after.

incorrect_examples.error: This is correct. No error here.

transformation_drills: Change '你需要休息' to a question.
production_drills: Ask a friend if they need help.
Say what you need to do today.
common_error_patterns: Using '需要' for moral advice
Negation placement error

repair_feedback.politeness: Use '需要' for practical needs, not for suggestions.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use '應該' for advice, '必須' for rules.

explanation_en: Use '必須' to express a strong obligation or necessity, often due to rules, laws, or unavoidable circumstances. It is stronger than '應該' and '需要'.
title: 必須 for Must
meaning: Must / have to
when_to_use: Expressing strong obligations
Talking about rules or requirements
Emphasizing necessity
when_not_to_use: For polite advice (use 應該)
For personal needs (use 需要)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, '必須' is used in formal contexts like signs, rules, or serious advice. It can sound too strong in casual conversation.
word_order_notes: Place '必須' before the main verb. Negation: '不必' or '不用' (don't have to), not '不必須'.
english_contrast: English 'must' is similar, but Chinese '必須' is less common in spoken advice; '應該' is more polite.
korean_contrast: Korean uses '-아/어야 한다' for must, similar to '必須', but '必須' is more formal.

correct_examples.text: 你必須早點睡覺，明天要上班。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū zǎo diǎn shuìjiào, míngtiān yào shàngbān.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must go to bed early; you have to work tomorrow.

correct_examples.text: 我們必須遵守公司的規定。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bìxū zūnshǒu gōngsī de guīdìng.
correct_examples.translation_en: We must follow the company's rules.

correct_examples.text: 你必須帶悠遊卡，不然不能坐捷運。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū dài yōuyóukǎ, bùrán bù néng zuò jiéyùn.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must bring your EasyCard, otherwise you can't take the MRT.

correct_examples.text: 你必須先結帳，然後才能拿發票。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū xiān jiézhàng, ránhòu cáinéng ná fāpiào.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must pay first, then you can get the receipt.

correct_examples.text: 你必須說中文，這樣才能進步。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū shuō Zhōngwén, zhèyàng cáinéng jìnbù.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must speak Chinese; only then can you improve.

correct_examples.text: 你必須吃藥，不然感冒不會好。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū chī yào, bùrán gǎnmào bù huì hǎo.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must take medicine, otherwise your cold won't get better.

correct_examples.text: 你必須帶傘，因為今天會下雨。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū dài sǎn, yīnwèi jīntiān huì xiàyǔ.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must bring an umbrella because it will rain today.

correct_examples.text: 你必須先預約，不然可能沒有座位。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ bìxū xiān yùyuē, bùrán kěnéng méiyǒu zuòwèi.
correct_examples.translation_en: You must make a reservation first, otherwise there might not be seats.

drill_examples.text: 你必須說中文，這樣才能進步。

negative_examples.error: Negation of '必須' is '不必' or '不用', not '不必須'.

incorrect_examples.error: This is correct if it's a strong necessity. No error here.

transformation_drills: Change '你必須早點到' to negative using '不必'.
Change '我們必須遵守規定' to a question.
production_drills: Tell a friend they must wear a mask in the hospital.

Using '必須' for polite advice

repair_feedback.word_order: Use '不必' for negation, not '不必須'.
repair_feedback.politeness: Use '應該' for polite advice, '必須' for strong rules.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use '需要' for personal needs.

explanation_en: Use '常常' (chángcháng) to express that an action happens frequently or often. It is placed before the verb phrase.
title: Expressing Frequency with 常常
meaning: Often do something
when_to_use: When talking about hobbies you do regularly
When describing routines or habits
when_not_to_use: For actions that happen only once or rarely
In negative sentences (use 不常 instead)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 常常 is commonly used in casual conversation about free time activities. It is neutral and polite.
word_order_notes: 常常 must come before the verb, not at the end of the sentence.
english_contrast: In English, 'often' can be placed before or after the verb (e.g., 'I often play basketball' or 'I play basketball often'). In Chinese, 常常 always goes before the verb.
korean_contrast: In Korean, '자주' (jaju) is similar and also placed before the verb, but Korean word order is SOV, while Chinese is SVO.

correct_examples.text: 我常常在週末打籃球。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ chángcháng zài zhōumò dǎ lánqiú.
correct_examples.translation_en: I often play basketball on weekends.

correct_examples.text: 他常常看小說，很少看漫畫。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā chángcháng kàn xiǎoshuō, hěn shǎo kàn mànhuà.
correct_examples.translation_en: He often reads novels, but rarely reads comics.

correct_examples.text: 我們有時候一起騎腳踏車去公園。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yǒu shíhou yīqǐ qí jiǎotàchē qù gōngyuán.
correct_examples.translation_en: Sometimes we ride bicycles together to the park.

correct_examples.text: 她常常聽廣播，也喜歡看演唱會。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā chángcháng tīng guǎngbò, yě xǐhuān kàn yǎnchànghuì.
correct_examples.translation_en: She often listens to the radio and also likes to watch concerts.

correct_examples.text: 我弟弟常常打電動，從來不看書。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ dìdi chángcháng dǎ diàndòng, cónglái bù kàn shū.
correct_examples.translation_en: My younger brother often plays video games and never reads books.

correct_examples.text: 放假的時候，我們常常去露營。
correct_examples.pinyin: Fàngjià de shíhou, wǒmen chángcháng qù lùyíng.
correct_examples.translation_en: During holidays, we often go camping.

correct_examples.text: 她常常彈鋼琴，有時候也彈吉他。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā chángcháng tán gāngqín, yǒu shíhou yě tán jítā.
correct_examples.translation_en: She often plays the piano, and sometimes also plays the guitar.

correct_examples.text: 我爸爸常常釣魚，他覺得很好玩。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ bàba chángcháng diào yú, tā juéde hěn hǎowán.
correct_examples.translation_en: My father often goes fishing; he thinks it's a lot of fun.

drill_examples.translation_ko: 우리는 가끔 함께 자전거를 타고 공원에 갑니다.

drill_examples.text: 她常常聽廣播，也喜歡看演唱會。

drill_examples.text: 我弟弟常常打電動，從來不看書。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ dìdi chángcháng dǎ diàndòng, cónglái bù kàn shū.

drill_examples.text: 放假的時候，我們常常去露營。
drill_examples.pinyin: Fàngjià de shíhou, wǒmen chángcháng qù lùyíng.
drill_examples.translation_en: During holidays, we often go camping.

drill_examples.text: 她常常彈鋼琴，有時候也彈吉他。

drill_examples.text: 我爸爸常常釣魚，他覺得很好玩。

drill_examples.translation_en: My father often goes fishing; he thinks it's a lot of fun.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 아버지는 자주 낚시를 가십니다. 아버지는 그것이 아주 재미있다고 생각하십니다.

negative_examples.error: Incorrect: 常常 is not used with negatives. Use 不常 instead.

incorrect_examples.error: Incorrect: 常常 must come before the verb, not after the object.

transformation_drills: Change '我打籃球' to '我常常打籃球' to add frequency.
Change '他看小說' to '他常常看小說'.
production_drills: Say you often listen to music.
Say your friend often plays tennis.
common_error_patterns: Placing 常常 after the verb or object
Using 常常 with negative verbs

repair_feedback.word_order: Remember: 常常 goes before the verb, e.g., '我常常看小說'.
repair_feedback.politeness: This structure is neutral and polite in all contexts.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 常常 for frequent actions; for rare actions, use 很少.

explanation_en: Use '有時候' (yǒu shíhou) to express that an action happens sometimes or occasionally. It is placed before the verb phrase.
title: Expressing Occasional Actions with 有時候
meaning: Sometimes do something
when_to_use: When talking about hobbies you do occasionally
When describing activities that are not regular
when_not_to_use: For actions that are always true
For actions that never happen (use 從來不 instead)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 有時候 is very common in casual speech. It can also be used at the beginning of a sentence for emphasis.
word_order_notes: 有時候 can be placed at the beginning of the sentence or before the verb. Both are correct.
english_contrast: In English, 'sometimes' can be placed at the beginning, middle, or end of a sentence. In Chinese, 有時候 is usually at the beginning or before the verb.
korean_contrast: In Korean, '가끔' (gakkeum) is similar and can be placed at the beginning or before the verb, similar to Chinese.

correct_examples.text: 我週末有時候會去打籃球。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ zhōumò yǒu shíhou huì qù dǎ lánqiú.
correct_examples.translation_en: I sometimes go play basketball on weekends.

correct_examples.text: 他平時很少看小說，有時候會看漫畫。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā píngshí hěn shǎo kàn xiǎoshuō, yǒu shíhou huì kàn mànhuà.
correct_examples.translation_en: He rarely reads novels on weekdays, but sometimes reads comics.

correct_examples.text: 我們有時候會一起去爬山，很好玩。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yǒu shíhou huì yīqǐ qù páshān, hěn hǎowán.
correct_examples.translation_en: We sometimes go hiking together; it's a lot of fun.

correct_examples.text: 放假的時候，我常常在家種花，有時候也做手工。
correct_examples.pinyin: Fàngjià de shíhou, wǒ chángcháng zài jiā zhòng huā, yǒu shíhou yě zuò shǒugōng.
correct_examples.translation_en: During holidays, I often plant flowers at home, and sometimes I also do crafts.

correct_examples.text: 你平時有什麼休閒興趣？我喜歡聽廣播和看展覽。
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ píngshí yǒu shénme xiūxián xìngqù? Wǒ xǐhuān tīng guǎngbò hé kàn zhǎnlǎn.
correct_examples.translation_en: What leisure interests do you usually have? I like listening to the radio and visiting exhibitions.

correct_examples.text: 他從來不打電動，可是有時候會看球賽。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā cónglái bù dǎ diàndòng, kěshì yǒu shíhou huì kàn qiúsài.
correct_examples.translation_en: He never plays video games, but sometimes he watches ball games.

correct_examples.text: 我妹妹有時候會彈鋼琴，她最喜歡彈吉他了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ mèimei yǒu shíhou huì tán gāngqín, tā zuì xǐhuān tán jítā le.
correct_examples.translation_en: My younger sister sometimes plays the piano, but she likes playing the guitar the most.

correct_examples.text: 我們要不要改天一起去釣魚？我最近有時候會去。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào bù yào gǎitiān yīqǐ qù diàoyú? Wǒ zuìjìn yǒu shíhou huì qù.
correct_examples.translation_en: Shall we go fishing together another day? I sometimes go recently.

drill_examples.translation_ko: 우리 다음에 같이 낚시하러 갈래요? 저는 요즘 가끔 갑니다.

negative_examples.error: Incorrect: 有時候 is not used with 沒有. Use 不常 or 很少 instead.

incorrect_examples.error: Incorrect: 有時候 should come before the verb or at the beginning of the sentence.

transformation_drills: Change '我爬山' to '我有时候爬山' to add occasional frequency.
Change '他聽廣播' to '他有时候聽廣播'.
production_drills: Say you sometimes play chess.
Say your friend sometimes goes camping.
common_error_patterns: Placing 有時候 after the verb or object
Using 有時候 in negative sentences

repair_feedback.word_order: Place 有時候 at the beginning or before the verb, e.g., '有時候我爬山' or '我有时候爬山'.
repair_feedback.politeness: This structure is neutral and polite.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 有時候 for occasional actions; for frequent actions, use 常常.

explanation_en: Use '喜歡' (xǐhuān) to express liking or enjoying an activity. It is followed directly by a verb phrase.
title: Expressing Hobbies with 喜歡
meaning: Like to do something
when_to_use: When talking about hobbies or activities you enjoy
When asking about someone's interests
when_not_to_use: For actions you dislike (use 不喜歡 instead)
For actions you do out of necessity (use 需要 instead)
pragmatic_notes: In Taiwan, 喜歡 is very common for discussing hobbies. It can be used with both verbs and nouns (e.g., 喜歡看小說, 喜歡咖啡).
word_order_notes: 喜歡 must be followed by a verb or noun. Do not place a subject between 喜歡 and the verb.
english_contrast: In English, 'like' can be followed by a gerund or infinitive (e.g., 'I like reading' or 'I like to read'). In Chinese, 喜歡 is always followed by a verb without any change.
korean_contrast: In Korean, '좋아하다' (joahada) is similar and is followed by a noun with the object particle, but Chinese uses a verb directly.

correct_examples.text: 我喜歡打籃球，也喜歡踢足球。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān dǎ lánqiú, yě xǐhuān tī zúqiú.
correct_examples.translation_en: I like playing basketball and also like playing soccer.

correct_examples.text: 你週末常常做什麼休閒活動？
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ zhōumò chángcháng zuò shénme xiūxián huódòng?
correct_examples.translation_en: What leisure activities do you often do on weekends?

correct_examples.text: 他喜歡下棋，可是我不會。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān xià qí, kěshì wǒ bù huì.
correct_examples.translation_en: He likes playing chess, but I don't know how.

correct_examples.text: 我們要不要一起去爬山？
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào bù yào yīqǐ qù pá shān?
correct_examples.translation_en: Do you want to go hiking together?

correct_examples.text: 她喜歡看小說，有時候也看漫畫。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān kàn xiǎoshuō, yǒu shíhou yě kàn mànhuà.
correct_examples.translation_en: She likes reading novels, and sometimes also reads comics.

correct_examples.text: 我很少打電動，因為太忙了。
correct_examples.pinyin: Wǒ hěn shǎo dǎ diàndòng, yīnwèi tài máng le.
correct_examples.translation_en: I rarely play video games because I'm too busy.

correct_examples.text: 你喜歡聽廣播還是看演唱會？
correct_examples.pinyin: Nǐ xǐhuān tīng guǎngbò háishì kàn yǎnchànghuì?
correct_examples.translation_en: Do you like listening to the radio or going to concerts?

correct_examples.text: 他從來不運動，只喜歡看球賽。
correct_examples.pinyin: Tā cónglái bù yùndòng, zhǐ xǐhuān kàn qiúsài.
correct_examples.translation_en: He never exercises; he only likes watching ball games.

drill_examples.text: 我喜歡打籃球，也喜歡踢足球。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān dǎ lánqiú, yě xǐhuān tī zúqiú.
drill_examples.translation_en: I like playing basketball and also like playing soccer.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 농구하는 것을 좋아하고, 축구하는 것도 좋아합니다.

drill_examples.text: 你週末常常做什麼休閒活動？
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ zhōumò chángcháng zuò shénme xiūxián huódòng?
drill_examples.translation_en: What leisure activities do you often do on weekends?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 주말에 자주 어떤 여가 활동을 하시나요?

drill_examples.text: 他喜歡下棋，可是我不會。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān xià qí, kěshì wǒ bù huì.
drill_examples.translation_en: He likes playing chess, but I don't know how.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 체스 두는 것을 좋아하지만, 저는 할 줄 모릅니다.

drill_examples.text: 我們要不要一起去爬山？
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào bù yào yīqǐ qù pá shān?
drill_examples.translation_en: Do you want to go hiking together?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 우리 같이 등산하러 갈래요?

drill_examples.text: 她喜歡看小說，有時候也看漫畫。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān kàn xiǎoshuō, yǒu shíhou yě kàn mànhuà.
drill_examples.translation_en: She likes reading novels, and sometimes also reads comics.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그녀는 소설 읽는 것을 좋아하고, 가끔 만화도 봅니다.

drill_examples.text: 我很少打電動，因為太忙了。
drill_examples.pinyin: Wǒ hěn shǎo dǎ diàndòng, yīnwèi tài máng le.
drill_examples.translation_en: I rarely play video games because I'm too busy.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 저는 게임을 거의 하지 않는데, 너무 바쁘기 때문입니다.

drill_examples.text: 你喜歡聽廣播還是看演唱會？
drill_examples.pinyin: Nǐ xǐhuān tīng guǎngbò háishì kàn yǎnchànghuì?
drill_examples.translation_en: Do you like listening to the radio or going to concerts?
drill_examples.translation_ko: 라디오 듣는 것을 좋아하세요, 아니면 콘서트 보는 것을 좋아하세요?

drill_examples.text: 他從來不運動，只喜歡看球賽。
drill_examples.pinyin: Tā cónglái bù yùndòng, zhǐ xǐhuān kàn qiúsài.
drill_examples.translation_en: He never exercises; he only likes watching ball games.
drill_examples.translation_ko: 그는 운동을 전혀 하지 않고, 경기 보는 것만 좋아합니다.

negative_examples.error: Incorrect: 喜歡 cannot be negated with 不 after it. Use 不喜歡 instead.

incorrect_examples.error: Incorrect: 喜歡 is followed by the verb phrase; frequency words like 常常 go before 喜歡 or after the verb.

transformation_drills: Change '我打籃球' to '我喜歡打籃球' to express enjoyment.
Change '他看小說' to '他喜歡看小說'.
production_drills: Say you like to play tennis.
Say your friend likes to watch baseball games.
common_error_patterns: Placing frequency words between 喜歡 and the verb
Using 喜歡 with negative verbs incorrectly

repair_feedback.word_order: Place 喜歡 directly before the verb, e.g., '我喜歡打籃球'.
repair_feedback.politeness: This structure is neutral and polite for discussing interests.
repair_feedback.vocabulary: Use 喜歡 for enjoyment; for necessity, use 需要.
